---
title: "Skill Trends in East Asia"
subtitle: "Insights from Online Job Postings"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: right
    smooth-scroll: true
    code-fold: show
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>
      :root {
        --bg-soft: #f8fafc;
        --ink-strong: #0f172a;
        --ink-mid: #334155;
        --card-border: #e2e8f0;
        --brand: #2563eb;
        --brand-2: #0891b2;
        --brand-3: #7c3aed;
      }

      body {
        color: var(--ink-mid);
        background: linear-gradient(180deg, #f8fbff 0%, #ffffff 40%);
        font-feature-settings: "ss01", "cv01";
      }

      #quarto-document-content p,
      #quarto-document-content li,
      #quarto-document-content dd {
        text-align: justify;
        text-justify: inter-word;
      }

      #title-block-header {
        margin-bottom: 1.2rem;
      }

      #title-block-header .title {
        font-size: clamp(2.4rem, 4.2vw, 3.35rem);
        line-height: 1.06;
        letter-spacing: -0.03em;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
      }

      #title-block-header .subtitle,
      #title-block-header p.subtitle {
        font-size: clamp(1.25rem, 2vw, 1.55rem);
        line-height: 1.2;
        color: #475569;
        margin-top: 0.45rem;
      }

      .report-eyebrow {
        display: block;
        color: #94a3b8;
        letter-spacing: 0.16em;
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 0.76rem;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
      }

      .hero {
        border: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        background: radial-gradient(1200px 280px at 0% 0%, #dbeafe 0%, #f8fafc 55%, #ffffff 100%);
        border-radius: 18px;
        padding: 1.4rem 1.2rem 1rem 1.2rem;
        margin: 0.2rem 0 1.2rem 0;
        box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.06);
      }

      .hero h2 {
        margin-top: 0.2rem;
        margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        letter-spacing: -0.01em;
      }

      .hero p {
        margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
        text-align: justify;
      }

      .kpi-grid {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(150px, 1fr));
        gap: 0.8rem;
        margin: 0.8rem 0 1.4rem 0;
      }

      .kpi-card {
        background: white;
        border: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        border-radius: 14px;
        padding: 0.75rem 0.9rem;
        box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.05);
      }

      .kpi-label {
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        color: #64748b;
        margin-bottom: 0.2rem;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.04em;
      }

      .kpi-value {
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 1.3rem;
        line-height: 1.2;
      }

      .section-card {
        background: #fff;
        border: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        border-radius: 16px;
        padding: 1rem 1rem 0.4rem 1rem;
        margin: 0.8rem 0 1rem 0;
        box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.04);
      }

      .step-detail {
        border-left: 3px solid #cbd5e1;
        padding: 0.6rem 0 0.6rem 1.2rem;
        margin: 1.2rem 0;
      }

      .step-detail h4 {
        font-size: 1.05rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        color: #0f172a;
      }

      .step-detail .step-badge {
        display: inline-block;
        font-size: 0.72rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        letter-spacing: 0.04em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        padding: 0.15rem 0.55rem;
        border-radius: 999px;
        margin-right: 0.4rem;
        vertical-align: middle;
      }

      .step-detail .step-badge-blue   { background: #e0f2fe; color: #0369a1; }
      .step-detail .step-badge-amber  { background: #fef3c7; color: #b45309; }
      .step-detail .step-badge-teal   { background: #ccfbf1; color: #0d9488; }
      .step-detail .step-badge-violet { background: #ede9fe; color: #7c3aed; }
      .step-detail .step-badge-indigo { background: #dbeafe; color: #1d4ed8; }
      .step-detail .step-badge-green  { background: #dcfce7; color: #15803d; }

      .cell[data-engine="mermaid"] .cell-output-display,
      .quarto-figure {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        align-items: center;
      }

      .pipeline-jump-note {
        margin-top: 0.25rem;
        margin-bottom: 0.2rem;
        font-size: 0.88rem;
        color: #64748b;
      }

      .table caption {
        caption-side: bottom;
        color: #64748b;
        font-size: 0.85rem;
        padding-top: 0.5rem;
      }

      .table thead th {
        background: #f1f5f9;
        color: #0f172a;
        border-bottom: 1px solid #cbd5e1;
      }

      .table tbody tr:hover {
        background: #f8fafc;
      }

      .market-table th {
        user-select: none;
        cursor: pointer;
        white-space: nowrap;
      }

      .market-table th.active-sort {
        color: var(--ink-strong);
      }

      .market-table td,
      .market-table th {
        vertical-align: middle;
      }

      .market-row-high td {
        background: #f8fbff;
      }

      .market-row-upper_middle td {
        background: #fffdf7;
      }

      .market-row-lower_middle td {
        background: #f7fdfa;
      }

      .market-row-benchmark td {
        background: #fbf9ff;
      }

      .market-table tbody tr:hover td {
        filter: brightness(0.985);
      }

      .source-note {
        margin-top: 0.95rem;
        padding-top: 0.8rem;
        border-top: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
      }

      .source-note-label {
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.05em;
        color: #64748b;
        margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
      }

      .source-links {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.7rem 1.4rem;
      }

      .source-link {
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: baseline;
        gap: 0.28rem;
        color: #0f6cbd;
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 1rem;
        line-height: 1.25;
        text-decoration: none;
        border-bottom: 2px solid rgba(15, 108, 189, 0.28);
        padding-bottom: 0.08rem;
        transition: color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease;
      }

      .source-link:hover,
      .source-link:focus {
        color: #0a5aa0;
        border-bottom-color: rgba(10, 90, 160, 0.5);
      }

      .source-link-ext {
        color: #7fb0d8;
        font-size: 0.9em;
      }

      .paper-link {
        position: relative;
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: baseline;
        gap: 0.28rem;
        color: #0f6cbd;
        font-weight: 700;
        line-height: 1.25;
        text-decoration: none;
        border-bottom: 2px solid rgba(15, 108, 189, 0.28);
        padding-bottom: 0.02rem;
        transition: color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease;
      }

      .paper-link:hover,
      .paper-link:focus {
        color: #0a5aa0;
        border-bottom-color: rgba(10, 90, 160, 0.5);
      }

      .paper-link:hover,
      .paper-link:focus-visible {
        z-index: 20;
      }

      .paper-tooltip {
        position: absolute;
        left: 0;
        top: calc(100% + 10px);
        z-index: 100;
        visibility: hidden;
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-4px);
        transition: opacity 160ms ease, transform 160ms ease, visibility 0s linear 160ms;
        box-sizing: border-box;
        min-width: 14rem;
        max-width: min(26rem, 92vw);
        padding: 0.65rem 0.85rem;
        border-radius: 10px;
        border: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        background: #fff;
        color: var(--ink-mid);
        font-size: 0.8125rem;
        font-weight: 500;
        line-height: 1.5;
        text-align: left;
        text-decoration: none;
        box-shadow: 0 12px 36px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12), 0 0 0 1px rgba(226, 232, 240, 0.9);
        pointer-events: none;
      }

      .paper-tooltip::before {
        content: "";
        position: absolute;
        left: 1rem;
        top: -6px;
        width: 10px;
        height: 10px;
        background: #fff;
        border-left: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        border-top: 1px solid var(--card-border);
        transform: rotate(45deg);
      }

      .paper-tooltip em {
        font-style: italic;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
      }

      .paper-link:hover .paper-tooltip,
      .paper-link:focus-visible .paper-tooltip {
        visibility: visible;
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
        transition: opacity 160ms ease, transform 160ms ease, visibility 0s;
      }

      @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
        .paper-tooltip {
          transition: none;
        }
      }

      .modal-link-btn {
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: center;
        gap: 0.35rem;
        margin-top: 0.7rem;
        padding: 0.5rem 0.85rem;
        border-radius: 999px;
        border: 1px solid #cfe0f3;
        background: #f8fbff;
        color: #0f6cbd;
        font-weight: 700;
        text-decoration: none;
        cursor: pointer;
      }

      .modal-link-btn:hover,
      .modal-link-btn:focus {
        background: #eef6ff;
        color: #0a5aa0;
        border-color: #b8d2ee;
      }

      .pill {
        display: inline-block;
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: 0.22rem 0.6rem;
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        font-weight: 600;
      }

      .pill-row {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.35rem;
        margin: 0.35rem 0 0.2rem 0;
      }

      .skill-pill-row {
        display: inline-flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.28rem;
        vertical-align: middle;
      }

      .skill-pill-common,
      .skill-pill-distinctive {
        display: inline-block;
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: 0.18rem 0.52rem;
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        line-height: 1.25;
        font-weight: 600;
        white-space: nowrap;
      }

      .skill-pill-common {
        background: #dbeafe;
        color: #1d4ed8;
      }

      .skill-pill-distinctive {
        background: #ffedd5;
        color: #9a3412;
      }

      .pill-high { background: #dbeafe; color: #1e3a8a; }
      .pill-upper { background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; }
      .pill-lower { background: #ccfbf1; color: #115e59; }
      .pill-benchmark {
        color: #5b21b6;
        border: 1px solid #c4b5fd;
        background: repeating-linear-gradient(
          -45deg,
          #f5f3ff,
          #f5f3ff 6px,
          #ede9fe 6px,
          #ede9fe 12px
        );
      }

      .map-tooltip {
        position: absolute;
        z-index: 20;
        pointer-events: none;
        opacity: 0;
        min-width: 220px;
        max-width: 280px;
        padding: 0.7rem 0.8rem;
        border-radius: 12px;
        border: 1px solid #dbe3f0;
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.97);
        box-shadow: 0 14px 34px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.14);
        backdrop-filter: blur(6px);
      }

      .map-tooltip-title {
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 0.95rem;
        margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
      }

      .map-tooltip-row {
        font-size: 0.82rem;
        color: var(--ink-mid);
        margin: 0.12rem 0;
      }

      .map-tooltip-label {
        color: #64748b;
        font-weight: 600;
      }

      .map-country-active {
        stroke: #0f172a !important;
        stroke-width: 1.2 !important;
        filter: brightness(0.98);
      }

      .map-controls {
        position: absolute;
        top: 12px;
        left: 12px;
        z-index: 15;
        display: flex;
        gap: 0.4rem;
      }

      .map-control-btn {
        border: 1px solid #dbe3f0;
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.94);
        color: #0f172a;
        border-radius: 10px;
        min-width: 2.2rem;
        height: 2.2rem;
        padding: 0 0.65rem;
        font-size: 0.95rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        line-height: 1;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08);
        backdrop-filter: blur(6px);
        cursor: pointer;
      }

      .map-control-btn:hover,
      .map-control-btn:focus {
        background: #ffffff;
        border-color: #bfd2ea;
      }

      .pipeline-diagram {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr));
        gap: 0.8rem;
        margin: 1.6rem 0 1rem 0;
        padding: 1rem;
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f8fafc 0%, #f1f5f9 100%);
        border-radius: 14px;
        border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
      }

      .pipeline-step {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        align-items: center;
        text-align: center;
        justify-content: flex-start;
        min-width: 165px;
        max-width: 185px;
        flex: 1;
        padding: 0.9rem 0.8rem 0.95rem 0.8rem;
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.78);
        border: 1px solid rgba(226, 232, 240, 0.95);
        border-radius: 16px;
        box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.05);
        transition: transform 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease;
        cursor: pointer;
      }

      .pipeline-step:hover,
      .pipeline-step:focus-within {
        transform: translateY(-3px) scale(1.025);
        box-shadow: 0 18px 34px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.10);
        border-color: #cbd5e1;
      }

      .pipeline-step:focus-visible {
        outline: 3px solid rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.22);
        outline-offset: 2px;
      }

      .pipeline-step-top {
        width: 100%;
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: space-between;
        gap: 0.7rem;
        margin-bottom: 0.7rem;
      }

      .pipeline-step-number {
        display: inline-flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: center;
        min-width: 2.05rem;
        height: 2.05rem;
        padding: 0 0.45rem;
        border-radius: 999px;
        border: 1px solid #dbe3f0;
        background: #ffffff;
        color: #64748b;
        font-size: 0.73rem;
        font-weight: 800;
        letter-spacing: 0.08em;
        box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8);
        transition: transform 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease, color 160ms ease;
      }

      .pipeline-icon {
        width: 60px;
        height: 60px;
        border-radius: 18px;
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: center;
        margin-bottom: 0.7rem;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08);
        transition: transform 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease, filter 160ms ease;
      }

      .pipeline-step:hover .pipeline-icon,
      .pipeline-step:focus-within .pipeline-icon {
        transform: scale(1.06);
        box-shadow: 0 14px 28px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12);
        filter: saturate(1.06) brightness(1.02);
      }

      .pipeline-step:hover .pipeline-step-number,
      .pipeline-step:focus-within .pipeline-step-number {
        transform: scale(1.04);
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        border-color: #cbd5e1;
        box-shadow: 0 6px 14px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.9);
      }

      .pipeline-label {
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 0.96rem;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        line-height: 1.22;
        margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
        min-height: 2.35rem;
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: center;
      }

      .pipeline-detail {
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        color: #64748b;
        line-height: 1.45;
        padding: 0 0.15rem;
        flex: 1;
        display: flex;
        align-items: flex-start;
      }

      .compare-controls {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.8rem;
        margin: 0.3rem 0 1rem 0;
      }

      .compare-control {
        min-width: 100%;
      }

      .compare-control label {
        display: block;
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        color: #64748b;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.05em;
        margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
      }

      .view-toggle-group {
        display: inline-flex;
      }

      .view-toggle-btn {
        border: 1px solid #bfdbfe;
        background: linear-gradient(180deg, #ffffff 0%, #f8fbff 100%);
        color: #1d4ed8;
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: 0.48rem 0.9rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 0.84rem;
        line-height: 1;
        cursor: pointer;
        transition: background 140ms ease, color 140ms ease, border-color 140ms ease, box-shadow 140ms ease;
        box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.10);
      }

      .view-toggle-btn:hover,
      .view-toggle-btn:focus {
        border-color: #93c5fd;
        background: #eff6ff;
        color: #1d4ed8;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.14);
      }

      .view-toggle-btn.active {
        background: #eff6ff;
        border-color: #93c5fd;
        color: #1d4ed8;
        box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.12);
      }

      .country-button-group {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.55rem;
      }

      .country-group-wrap {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        gap: 1rem;
        align-items: stretch;
      }

      .country-group {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        gap: 0.4rem;
        width: 100%;
      }

      .country-group-label {
        font-size: 0.7rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.07em;
        color: #94a3b8;
        padding-left: 0.15rem;
      }

      .country-group-pills {
        display: flex;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: 0.45rem;
      }

      .country-pill {
        border: 1px solid #dbe3f0;
        background: #ffffff;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: 0.5rem 0.8rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        font-size: 0.9rem;
        line-height: 1;
        cursor: pointer;
        transition: background 140ms ease, color 140ms ease, border-color 140ms ease, transform 140ms ease, box-shadow 140ms ease;
        box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.04);
      }

      .country-pill:hover,
      .country-pill:focus {
        transform: translateY(-1px);
        border-color: #bfd2ea;
      }

      .country-pill.active {
        background: #eff6ff;
        border-color: #93c5fd;
        color: #1d4ed8;
        box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.12);
      }

      .country-pill.disabled {
        background: #f8fafc;
        color: #94a3b8;
        border-color: #e2e8f0;
        cursor: not-allowed;
        box-shadow: none;
      }

      .compare-helper {
        font-size: 0.78rem;
        color: #94a3b8;
      }

      .compare-note {
        font-size: 0.84rem;
        color: #64748b;
        margin: 0.2rem 0 0.9rem 0;
      }

      .compare-charts {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
        gap: 1rem;
      }

      .compare-panel-title {
        font-size: 1.05rem;
        font-weight: 800;
        color: var(--ink-strong);
        margin: 0.2rem 0 0.45rem 0;
      }

      .keyness-meta {
        font-size: 0.84rem;
        color: #64748b;
        margin: 0.15rem 0 0.95rem 0;
      }

      .keyness-table-wrap {
        overflow-x: auto;
      }

      .keyness-table td,
      .keyness-table th {
        white-space: nowrap;
      }

      .keyness-table td:first-child,
      .keyness-table th:first-child {
        white-space: normal;
      }

      .keyness-code {
        color: #94a3b8;
        font-weight: 700;
      }

      @media (max-width: 680px) {
        .pipeline-diagram {
          grid-template-columns: 1fr;
        }
        .pipeline-step {
          max-width: none;
          width: 100%;
        }
        .compare-controls {
          gap: 0.6rem;
        }
      }

      @media (min-width: 681px) and (max-width: 980px) {
        .pipeline-diagram {
          grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
        }
      }

      @media (max-width: 1100px) {
        .kpi-grid {
          grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(140px, 1fr));
        }
      }

      @media (max-width: 720px) {
        .kpi-grid {
          grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(140px, 1fr));
        }
      }

      @media (max-width: 600px) {
        .kpi-grid {
          grid-template-columns: 1fr;
        }
      }
    </style>
    <script>
      document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
        const title = document.querySelector("#title-block-header .title");
        if (title && !document.querySelector(".report-eyebrow")) {
          const tag = document.createElement("span");
          tag.className = "report-eyebrow";
          tag.textContent = "PROGRESS REPORT";
          title.parentNode.insertBefore(tag, title);
        }

        document.querySelectorAll(".pipeline-step[data-target]").forEach(function (step) {
          const activate = function () {
            const targetId = step.getAttribute("data-target");
            const target = document.getElementById(targetId);
            if (!target) return;
            target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" });
          };

          step.addEventListener("click", activate);
          step.addEventListener("keydown", function (event) {
            if (event.key === "Enter" || event.key === " ") {
              event.preventDefault();
              activate();
            }
          });
        });
      });
    </script>
---

::: {.hero}
## Executive Summary

This report analyses labour demand in East Asia through
the lens of online job advertisements, with a focus on how employers
signal required skills across occupations and markets. It combines
labour-market analysis with modern NLP methods, including automated
translation, sentence-level classification, and LLM-assisted skill
extraction, to turn unstructured vacancy text into comparable measures
of skill demand.

Using ESCO-based mappings, the report compares the composition of
requested skills and knowledge areas across countries, with special
focus on digital competencies and indicators of relative labour demand
derived from job posting intensity. Labour markets across East Asia are
undergoing
rapid transformation, with increasing digitization reshaping how
employers search for talent. Online job postings (OJPs) provide a useful
proxy for real-time labour demand, particularly for mid- and high-skill
occupations.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="kpi-grid">
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-label">Job Ads</div>
    <div class="kpi-value">1,860,159</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-label">Markets Analyzed</div>
    <div class="kpi-value">9 <span style="font-size:0.75rem;font-weight:500;color:#64748b;">East Asian</span></div>
    <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:#94a3b8;margin-top:0.15rem;">+ 3 benchmark</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-label">ESCO Skills</div>
    <div class="kpi-value">13.9K</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-label">Data Period</div>
    <div class="kpi-value" style="font-size:1rem; line-height:1.35;">Mar 2025 — Mar 2026</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Regional Coverage

### Countries covered

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

topojson = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/topojson-client@3/+esm")

world = fetch(
  "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/world-atlas@2/countries-110m.json"
).then(r => r.json())

// ISO 3166-1 numeric → label + income group + offer counts
countryMeta = ({
  "392": { name: "Japan",        group: "high",         iso3: "JPN", offers: 299669 },
  "410": { name: "South Korea",  group: "high",         iso3: "KOR", offers: 132148 },
  "158": { name: "Taiwan",       group: "high",         iso3: "TWN", offers: 55217 },
  "702": { name: "Singapore",    group: "high",         iso3: "SGP", offers: 138202 },
  "764": { name: "Thailand",     group: "upper_middle", iso3: "THA", offers: 57892 },
  "360": { name: "Indonesia",    group: "upper_middle", iso3: "IDN", offers: 113460 },
  "458": { name: "Malaysia",     group: "upper_middle", iso3: "MYS", offers: 171586 },
  "704": { name: "Vietnam",      group: "lower_middle", iso3: "VNM", offers: 145549 },
  "608": { name: "Philippines",  group: "lower_middle", iso3: "PHL", offers: 187818 },
  "356": { name: "India",        group: "benchmark",    iso3: "IND", offers: 224187 },
})

benchmarkMeta = ([
  { name: "Mexico", group: "benchmark", iso3: "MEX", offers: 199404 },
  { name: "Poland", group: "benchmark", iso3: "POL", offers: 135027 },
])

mapBenchmarkMetaById = ({
  "484": { name: "Mexico", group: "benchmark", iso3: "MEX", offers: 199404 },
  "616": { name: "Poland", group: "benchmark", iso3: "POL", offers: 135027 },
})

allMarketMeta = [
  ...Object.values(countryMeta),
  ...benchmarkMeta,
]

palette = ({
  high:         "#1d4ed8",
  upper_middle: "#f59e0b",
  lower_middle: "#0d9488",
  benchmark:    "#7c3aed",
  bg:        "#f1f5f9",
  border:    "#ffffff",
  ocean:     "#eff6ff",
  land:      "#e2e8f0",
})

// Label anchor positions [lon, lat] with nudge
labelAnchors = [
  { id: "392", lon: 140,  lat: 37,   dx: 8,  dy: 0   },
  { id: "410", lon: 128,  lat: 36,   dx: -45,dy: 12  },
  { id: "158", lon: 121,  lat: 23.5, dx: 8,  dy: 8   },
  { id: "764", lon: 101,  lat: 14,   dx: -10,dy: 16  },
  { id: "704", lon: 107,  lat: 16,   dx: 10, dy: 0   },
  { id: "458", lon: 109,  lat: 3,    dx: -50,dy: -8  },
  { id: "608", lon: 122,  lat: 12,   dx: 10, dy: 0   },
  { id: "702", lon: 104,  lat: 1.3,  dx: -55,dy: 8   },
  { id: "360", lon: 118,  lat: -2,   dx: 0,  dy: 14  },
  { id: "356", lon: 79,   lat: 22,   dx: -10,dy: 0   },
  { id: "484", lon: -102, lat: 23,   dx: 10, dy: 0   },
  { id: "616", lon: 19,   lat: 52,   dx: 8,  dy: 0   },
]

laborForceOverrides = ({
  TWN: { year: 2025, value: 12028000 },
})

laborForceByIso3 = Object.fromEntries(await Promise.all(
  [...new Set(allMarketMeta.map(d => d.iso3))].map(async iso3 => {
    if (laborForceOverrides[iso3]) {
      return [iso3, laborForceOverrides[iso3]]
    }
    const url = `https://api.worldbank.org/v2/country/${iso3}/indicator/SL.TLF.TOTL.IN?format=json&per_page=3`
    const data = await fetch(url).then(r => r.json())
    const row = (data?.[1] || []).find(d => d.value != null)
    return [iso3, row ? { year: Number(row.date), value: Number(row.value) } : null]
  })
))

gdpGrowthOverrides = ({
  TWN: { year: 2025, value: 8.63 },
})

gdpGrowthByIso3 = Object.fromEntries(await Promise.all(
  [...new Set(allMarketMeta.map(d => d.iso3))].map(async iso3 => {
    if (gdpGrowthOverrides[iso3]) {
      return [iso3, gdpGrowthOverrides[iso3]]
    }
    const url = `https://api.worldbank.org/v2/country/${iso3}/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.KD.ZG?format=json&per_page=3`
    const data = await fetch(url).then(r => r.json())
    const row = (data?.[1] || []).find(d => d.value != null)
    return [iso3, row ? { year: Number(row.date), value: Number(row.value) } : null]
  })
))

fmtInt = d3.format(",")
fmtRate = d3.format(".1f")

groupLabels = ({
  high: "High-income",
  upper_middle: "Upper-middle-income",
  lower_middle: "Lower-middle-income",
  benchmark: "Benchmark",
})

metricsFor = (meta) => {
  const lf = laborForceByIso3[meta.iso3]
  const gdp = gdpGrowthByIso3[meta.iso3]
  return {
    ...meta,
    income_group: groupLabels[meta.group],
    labor_force: lf?.value ?? null,
    labor_force_year: lf?.year ?? null,
    real_gdp_growth_2025: gdp?.value ?? null,
    real_gdp_growth_year: gdp?.year ?? null,
    offers_per_100k: (meta.offers != null && lf?.value)
      ? (meta.offers / lf.value) * 100000
      : null,
  }
}

countryRowsById = Object.fromEntries(
  Object.entries(countryMeta).map(([id, meta]) => [id, metricsFor(meta)])
)

mapRowsById = Object.fromEntries(
  [...Object.entries(countryMeta), ...Object.entries(mapBenchmarkMetaById)]
    .map(([id, meta]) => [id, metricsFor(meta)])
)

benchmarkRows = benchmarkMeta.map(metricsFor)

groupOrder = ({ high: 0, upper_middle: 1, lower_middle: 2, benchmark: 3 })

reportRows = [...Object.values(countryRowsById), ...benchmarkRows]
  .sort((a, b) => groupOrder[a.group] - groupOrder[b.group] || b.offers - a.offers)

tooltipHtml = (row) => {
  const offersText = row.offers == null ? "n/a" : fmtInt(row.offers)
  const lfText = row.labor_force == null ? "n/a" : fmtInt(row.labor_force)
  const per100k = row.offers_per_100k == null ? "n/a" : fmtRate(row.offers_per_100k)
  const gdpText = row.real_gdp_growth_2025 == null ? "n/a" : fmtRate(row.real_gdp_growth_2025)
  const lfYear = row.labor_force_year ? ` (${row.labor_force_year})` : ""
  return `
    <div class="map-tooltip-title">${row.name}</div>
    <div class="map-tooltip-row"><span class="map-tooltip-label">Income group:</span> ${row.income_group}</div>
    <div class="map-tooltip-row"><span class="map-tooltip-label">Job offers:</span> ${offersText}</div>
    <div class="map-tooltip-row"><span class="map-tooltip-label">Labor force${lfYear}:</span> ${lfText}</div>
    <div class="map-tooltip-row"><span class="map-tooltip-label">Offers per 100k LF:</span> ${per100k}</div>
    <div class="map-tooltip-row"><span class="map-tooltip-label">Real GDP growth (2025):</span> ${gdpText}%</div>
  `
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| fig-cap: "Analyzed countries grouped by income status."

{
  const W = 860, H = 560;
  const container = d3.create("div")
    .style("position", "relative")
    .style("max-width", "100%")
    .style("overflow", "hidden");

  const tooltip = container.append("div")
    .attr("class", "map-tooltip");

  const controls = container.append("div")
    .attr("class", "map-controls");

  const projection = d3.geoMercator()
    .center([108, 18])
    .scale(480)
    .translate([W / 2, H / 2]);

  const path = d3.geoPath(projection);

  const land = topojson.feature(world, world.objects.countries);

  const svg = container.append("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", [0, 0, W, H])
    .attr("width", W)
    .attr("height", H)
    .style("max-width", "100%")
    .style("height", "auto")
    .style("font-family", "system-ui, sans-serif");

  // Ocean and subtle background grid feel
  svg.append("rect")
    .attr("width", W).attr("height", H)
    .attr("fill", palette.ocean);

  const mapLayer = svg.append("g");

  // Stripe pattern for benchmark country (India)
  const defs = svg.append("defs");
  const hatch = defs.append("pattern")
    .attr("id", "benchmarkHatch")
    .attr("patternUnits", "userSpaceOnUse")
    .attr("width", 8)
    .attr("height", 8)
    .attr("patternTransform", "rotate(45)");

  hatch.append("rect")
    .attr("width", 8)
    .attr("height", 8)
    .attr("fill", "#f5f3ff");

  hatch.append("line")
    .attr("x1", 0).attr("y1", 0)
    .attr("x2", 0).attr("y2", 8)
    .attr("stroke", palette.benchmark)
    .attr("stroke-width", 2);

  const showTooltip = (event, row) => {
    const [x, y] = d3.pointer(event, container.node());
    const rect = container.node().getBoundingClientRect();
    const tooltipWidth = 250;
    const tooltipHeight = 120;
    const left = Math.min(rect.width - tooltipWidth - 12, Math.max(12, x + 14));
    const top = Math.min(rect.height - tooltipHeight - 12, Math.max(12, y + 14));
    tooltip
      .html(tooltipHtml(row))
      .style("left", `${left}px`)
      .style("top", `${top}px`)
      .style("opacity", 1);
  };

  const hideTooltip = () => {
    tooltip.style("opacity", 0);
  };

  const zoom = d3.zoom()
    .scaleExtent([0.15, 6])
    .on("zoom", (event) => {
      mapLayer.attr("transform", event.transform);
    });

  svg.call(zoom);

  controls.append("button")
    .attr("class", "map-control-btn")
    .attr("type", "button")
    .attr("title", "Zoom in")
    .text("+")
    .on("click", () => {
      svg.transition().duration(220).call(zoom.scaleBy, 1.35);
    });

  controls.append("button")
    .attr("class", "map-control-btn")
    .attr("type", "button")
    .attr("title", "Zoom out")
    .text("−")
    .on("click", () => {
      svg.transition().duration(220).call(zoom.scaleBy, 1 / 1.35);
    });

  controls.append("button")
    .attr("class", "map-control-btn")
    .attr("type", "button")
    .attr("title", "Reset view")
    .style("min-width", "auto")
    .text("Reset")
    .on("click", () => {
      svg.transition().duration(260).call(zoom.transform, d3.zoomIdentity);
    });

  // Countries
  const countryPaths = mapLayer.selectAll("path.country")
    .data(land.features)
    .join("path")
      .attr("class", "country")
      .attr("d", path)
      .attr("fill", d => {
        const m = mapRowsById[d.id];
        if (!m) return palette.land;
        return m.group === "benchmark" ? "url(#benchmarkHatch)" : palette[m.group];
      })
      .attr("stroke", palette.border)
      .attr("stroke-width", 0.5)
      .attr("opacity", d => mapRowsById[d.id] ? 1 : 0.6)
      .style("cursor", d => mapRowsById[d.id] ? "pointer" : "default");

  countryPaths
    .filter(d => mapRowsById[d.id])
    .on("mouseenter", function(event, d) {
      d3.select(this).classed("map-country-active", true);
      showTooltip(event, mapRowsById[d.id]);
    })
    .on("mousemove", function(event, d) {
      showTooltip(event, mapRowsById[d.id]);
    })
    .on("mouseleave", function() {
      d3.select(this).classed("map-country-active", false);
      hideTooltip();
    });

  // Small-country markers (Singapore is invisible at 110m)
  for (const anc of labelAnchors) {
    if (anc.id === "702") {
      const [cx, cy] = projection([anc.lon, anc.lat]);
      const m = mapRowsById[anc.id];
      mapLayer.append("circle")
        .attr("cx", cx).attr("cy", cy).attr("r", 4)
        .attr("fill", m.group === "benchmark" ? palette.benchmark : palette[m.group])
        .attr("stroke", palette.border)
        .attr("stroke-width", 1)
        .style("cursor", "pointer")
        .on("mouseenter", (event) => showTooltip(event, m))
        .on("mousemove", (event) => showTooltip(event, m))
        .on("mouseleave", hideTooltip);
    }
  }

  // Labels
  const labels = mapLayer.append("g").attr("class", "labels");

  for (const anc of labelAnchors) {
    const [x, y] = projection([anc.lon, anc.lat]);
    const m = mapRowsById[anc.id];
    if (!m) continue;

    labels.append("text")
      .attr("x", x + (anc.dx || 0))
      .attr("y", y + (anc.dy || 0))
      .attr("text-anchor", "start")
      .attr("font-size", "11px")
      .attr("font-weight", 600)
      .attr("fill", "#1e293b")
      .attr("paint-order", "stroke")
      .attr("stroke", "#ffffffcc")
      .attr("stroke-width", 3)
      .text(m.name);
  }

  // Legend
  const lg = svg.append("g")
    .attr("transform", `translate(20, ${H - 106})`);

  const items = [
    { label: "High-income economies", color: palette.high, hatch: false },
    { label: "Upper-middle-income economies", color: palette.upper_middle, hatch: false },
    { label: "Lower-middle-income economies", color: palette.lower_middle, hatch: false },
    { label: "Benchmark", color: palette.benchmark, hatch: true },
  ];

  items.forEach(({ label, color, hatch }, i) => {
    const marker = lg.append("rect")
      .attr("x", 0).attr("y", i * 22)
      .attr("width", 14).attr("height", 14)
      .attr("rx", 2)
      .attr("fill", color);
    if (hatch) {
      marker
        .attr("fill", "url(#benchmarkHatch)")
        .attr("stroke", color)
        .attr("stroke-width", 1);
    }
    lg.append("text")
      .attr("x", 20).attr("y", i * 22 + 12)
      .attr("font-size", "12px")
      .attr("fill", "#334155")
      .text(label);
  });

  return container.node();
}
```

<div class="pill-row">
<span class="pill pill-lower">Lower-middle-income: Vietnam, Philippines</span>
<span class="pill pill-upper">Upper-middle-income: Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia</span>
<span class="pill pill-high">High-income: Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Taiwan</span>
<span class="pill pill-benchmark">Benchmark: India, Mexico, Poland</span>
</div>

::: {.section-card}
### Relative labour demand

The table below summarizes the absolute number of job offers and the number of
offers per 100,000 people in the labor force. This allows us to compare
both the scale of observed hiring and its intensity relative to each
country's labour supply. Click a column header to sort the table.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const rows = reportRows.map(d => ({
    country: d.name,
    group: d.group,
    income_group: d.income_group,
    offers: d.offers,
    offers_per_100k: d.offers_per_100k,
    real_gdp_growth_2025: d.real_gdp_growth_2025,
  }));

  const table = d3.create("table")
    .attr("class", "table table-sm align-middle market-table");

  const columns = [
    { key: "country", label: "Country", type: "text" },
    { key: "income_group", label: "Income group", type: "text" },
    { key: "offers", label: "Job offers", type: "number" },
    { key: "offers_per_100k", label: "Offers per 100k labor force", type: "number" },
    { key: "real_gdp_growth_2025", label: "Real GDP growth (2025)", type: "number" },
  ];

  let sortKey = "income_group";
  let sortDirection = "asc";

  const thead = table.append("thead");
  const headerRow = thead.append("tr");
  const tbody = table.append("tbody");

  const sortRows = () => {
    const sorted = [...rows].sort((a, b) => {
      if (sortKey === "income_group") {
        const cmp = groupOrder[a.group] - groupOrder[b.group];
        if (cmp !== 0) return sortDirection === "asc" ? cmp : -cmp;
        return b.offers - a.offers;
      }
      const av = a[sortKey];
      const bv = b[sortKey];
      if (av == null && bv == null) return 0;
      if (av == null) return 1;
      if (bv == null) return -1;
      const cmp = columns.find(c => c.key === sortKey).type === "text"
        ? String(av).localeCompare(String(bv))
        : av - bv;
      return sortDirection === "asc" ? cmp : -cmp;
    });
    return sorted;
  };

  const renderBody = () => {
    tbody.selectAll("tr").remove();
    sortRows().forEach(row => {
      const tr = tbody.append("tr")
        .attr("class", `market-row-${row.group}`);
      tr.append("td").text(row.country);
      tr.append("td").text(row.income_group);
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").text(fmtInt(row.offers));
      tr.append("td")
        .style("text-align", "right")
        .text(row.offers_per_100k == null ? "n/a" : fmtRate(row.offers_per_100k));
      tr.append("td")
        .style("text-align", "right")
        .text(row.real_gdp_growth_2025 == null ? "n/a" : `${fmtRate(row.real_gdp_growth_2025)}%`);
    });
  };

  columns.forEach(col => {
    headerRow.append("th")
      .attr("class", col.key === sortKey ? "active-sort" : null)
      .text(`${col.label}${col.key === sortKey ? (sortDirection === "asc" ? " ↑" : " ↓") : ""}`)
      .on("click", function() {
        if (sortKey === col.key) {
          sortDirection = sortDirection === "asc" ? "desc" : "asc";
        } else {
          sortKey = col.key;
          sortDirection = col.type === "text" ? "asc" : "desc";
        }
        headerRow.selectAll("th")
          .attr("class", d => null)
          .text((d, i) => {
            const c = columns[i];
            return `${c.label}${c.key === sortKey ? (sortDirection === "asc" ? " ↑" : " ↓") : ""}`;
          });
        headerRow.selectAll("th")
          .filter((d, i) => columns[i].key === sortKey)
          .attr("class", "active-sort");
        renderBody();
      });
  });

  renderBody();

  return table.node();
}
```
:::

```{=html}
<div class="source-note">
  <div class="source-note-label">Macroeconomic sources</div>
  <div class="source-links">
    <a class="source-link" href="https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/SL.TLF.TOTL.IN" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
      World Bank labor force (2025)
      <span class="source-link-ext">↗</span>
    </a>
    <a class="source-link" href="https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.KD.ZG" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
      World Bank real GDP growth (2025)
      <span class="source-link-ext">↗</span>
    </a>
    <a class="source-link" href="https://eng.stat.gov.tw/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
      National Statistics, Republic of China (Taiwan, 2025)
      <span class="source-link-ext">↗</span>
    </a>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Data and Methodology

::: {.section-card}
### Data collection

We collect job advertisements from **Indeed** and **Careerjet**, two large
aggregators with country-specific sites across the markets covered in this
study. The analytical sample shown here comprises roughly **1.86 million**
postings covering March 2025 – March 2026. Collection ran **daily for a full
year** because high-volume portals often stop returning results after a limited
number of pages. Collecting every day reduced the risk of missing vacancies
that would no longer be reachable if the portals were queried less frequently.

```{=html}
<button type="button" class="modal-link-btn" data-bs-toggle="modal" data-bs-target="#dataCollectionModal">
  Read more
  <span aria-hidden="true">↗</span>
</button>

<div class="modal fade" id="dataCollectionModal" tabindex="-1" aria-labelledby="dataCollectionModalLabel" aria-hidden="true">
  <div class="modal-dialog modal-lg modal-dialog-scrollable">
    <div class="modal-content">
      <div class="modal-header">
        <h5 class="modal-title" id="dataCollectionModalLabel">Data collection details</h5>
        <button type="button" class="btn-close" data-bs-dismiss="modal" aria-label="Close"></button>
      </div>
      <div class="modal-body">
        <p>We use two aggregators: <strong>Indeed</strong>, the most established platform in the sample, and <strong>Careerjet</strong>, a newer but growing platform. We selected them because both provide dedicated country-level sites across the markets included in the analysis.</p>
        <p>For all country-platform combinations, we developed Python web scrapers that retrieve job postings and extract core information such as job title, job description, company name, salary, job and company location, posting date, contract type, remote-work status, industry, company size and revenue, and the original job-post URL.</p>
        <p>Because attribute availability varies across postings, we restrict the analytical sample to offers containing at least a job title, a job description, and the original URL. We additionally remove duplicates with identical titles and descriptions, both within each job board and across boards.</p>
        <p>Both portals impose practical result limits for broad queries. Indeed typically exposes up to 1,000 postings and Careerjet up to 2,000. To improve coverage, we run multiple searches by region within each country and monitor cases where the maximum visible result count is returned, since that suggests that additional postings may be omitted.</p>
        <p>The collection pipeline ran daily over a full year. This high-frequency collection schedule helped us capture changing posting intensity over time, while also reducing the risk that short-lived vacancies would be systematically missed.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

:::

::: {.section-card}
### Processing pipeline

Raw job postings arrive in different languages and contain noisy,
unstructured information. We transform them into a standardised analytic
dataset in six steps: we first identify the posting language, translate
non-English text into a common language, isolate skill-relevant
sentences, map extracted skills to ESCO, standardise job titles to ESCO
occupations using both titles and skill context for disambiguation, and
then enrich each posting with sector information by assigning ISIC/NACE
economic activity codes.

```{=html}
<div class="pipeline-diagram">
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-01" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 01">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">01</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#e0f2fe;color:#0369a1;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M6 4h9a3 3 0 0 1 3 3v6a3 3 0 0 1-3 3H9l-4 4v-4H6a3 3 0 0 1-3-3V7a3 3 0 0 1 3-3z"/><path d="M8 9h5"/><path d="M8 12h3"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Language<br>Detection</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Language identification for each posting, including mixed-language offers</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-02" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 02">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">02</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#fef3c7;color:#b45309;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 8h6"/><path d="M8 6v2c0 2.1-1 4.2-2.8 5.6"/><path d="M6 12c.8 1.2 2 2.2 3.4 2.8"/><path d="m13 7 6 10"/><path d="m19 7-6 10"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Translation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Gemma 3 Translate 4B converts non-English titles and descriptions into English</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-03" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 03">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">03</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#ccfbf1;color:#0d9488;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M4 6h16"/><path d="M7 12h10"/><path d="M10 18h4"/><circle cx="6.5" cy="6.5" r="1"/><circle cx="17.5" cy="12.5" r="1"/><circle cx="8.5" cy="18.5" r="1"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Skill-Sentence<br>Filtering</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Fine-tuned BERT-architecture classifier identifies skill-relevant sentences</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-04" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 04">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">04</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#ede9fe;color:#7c3aed;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="7"/><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="3"/><path d="M12 2v3"/><path d="M12 19v3"/><path d="M2 12h3"/><path d="M19 12h3"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">ESCO<br>Mapping</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Custom contrastive bi-encoder maps skill sentences to ESCO skill entries</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-05" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 05">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">05</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#dbeafe;color:#1d4ed8;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M7 4h10"/><path d="M9 8h6"/><path d="M12 8v9"/><path d="M8 20h8"/><path d="M5 12c1.2-1.4 2.8-2 4.7-2"/><path d="M19 12c-1.2-1.4-2.8-2-4.7-2"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Job Title<br>Standardisation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Job titles are mapped to ESCO occupations using titles and extracted skills as context for disambiguation</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step" data-target="pipeline-step-06" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="Jump to Step 06">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">06</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#dcfce7;color:#15803d;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M4 7h16"/><path d="M4 12h10"/><path d="M4 17h7"/><path d="M17 11c2.2 0 4 1.8 4 4s-1.8 4-4 4-4-1.8-4-4"/><path d="m17 13 1.4 1.4L21 12"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Data<br>Augmentation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">ISIC/NACE economic activity is assigned using the official ESCO-NACE crosswalk and job-ad context for disambiguation</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.pipeline-jump-note}
Click any card to jump to the detailed description of that step.
:::

Each step is described in detail below.

:::: {#pipeline-step-01 .step-detail}
#### [Step 01]{.step-badge .step-badge-blue} Language detection

Comparing skill demand across countries that operate in different
languages requires, as a first step, reliable identification of the
language in which each posting was written. This ensures that postings
already in English can proceed directly to analysis, while those in
other languages are routed to the appropriate translation track.

We apply a machine-learning-based language detector (<a class="paper-link" href="https://github.com/pemistahl/lingua-py" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Lingua<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>)
independently to both the job title and the full job description of
every posting. The detector covers all languages present in our sample,
including CJK scripts (Chinese, Japanese, Korean), Thai, Vietnamese,
and Latin-script languages (English, Spanish, Malay, Filipino, Polish).
Per-field detection allows us to handle the common case where a title
is in English but the body is in the local language. This is important
because mixed-language postings are common in online vacancies, and
Lingua performs well even when different parts of the same offer use
different languages.
::::

:::: {#pipeline-step-02 .step-detail}
#### [Step 02]{.step-badge .step-badge-amber} Translation

Cross-country skill comparison requires a common-language
representation of all job postings. With a corpus of nearly
**2 million job ads**, machine translation is not an optional
enhancement but a practical necessity: without it, large-scale
cross-country comparison would not be feasible in a consistent and
timely manner. Recent research indicates that LLM-based translation
systems can deliver high-quality machine translation at scale,
supporting the efficient processing of large datasets such as the one
used in this study (see
<a class="paper-link" href="https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1007/978-3-031-44693-1_30" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Huang et al. (2023)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>).

Postings already detected as English pass through unchanged. All
remaining postings are translated with <a class="paper-link" href="https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/translategemma/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Gemma 3 Translate 4B<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>, an open
source multilingual translation model designed for high-quality
instruction following and strong performance across typologically
different languages. Both **job titles** and **job descriptions** are
translated into English and stored alongside the originals to allow
back-referencing when analysts need to inspect the source text. This
ensures that all downstream steps, including title standardisation,
operate on a common-language representation.

To validate translation quality, we conducted a **back-translation**
exercise on a small random sample of Polish postings. In this check,
texts were translated into English and then translated back into
Polish, allowing us to verify that the meaning of skills, occupations,
and task descriptions was preserved with minimal drift.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Translation routing"
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'htmlLabels': true, 'nodeSpacing': 40, 'rankSpacing': 50}} }%%
flowchart LR
    A["Raw posting"] --> B{"Language<br/>detected?"}
    B -->|"Already English"| C["Keep original text"]
    B -->|"Other language"| D["Gemma 3 Translate 4B"]
    D -->|"title + description"| E["Translated English text"]
    C --> F["Unified English corpus"]
    E --> F
```
::::

:::: {#pipeline-step-03 .step-detail}
#### [Step 03]{.step-badge .step-badge-teal} Skill-sentence filtering

Job descriptions typically mix skill requirements with administrative
content such as benefits, company history, or application instructions.
Isolating the skill-relevant sentences before downstream mapping
reduces noise and improves the precision of the extracted skill
profiles.

We use a fine-tuned BERT classifier based on the
<a class="paper-link" href="https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.1145/3672608.3707960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Malandri et al. (2025)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>
SkillLess approach and the
<a class="paper-link" href="https://huggingface.co/serino28/BERT-OJA-SkillLess" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">BERT-OJA-SkillLess<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>.
The model performs binary sentence-level classification,
labelling each sentence as either skill-relevant or non-skill.
In our sample, approximately 40--60 % of description text is
filtered out as non-skill content. Only the retained sentences
proceed to the ESCO mapping step.
::::

:::: {#pipeline-step-04 .step-detail}
#### [Step 04]{.step-badge .step-badge-violet} ESCO skill mapping

The European Skills, Competences, Qualifications and Occupations
(<a class="paper-link" href="https://esco.ec.europa.eu/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ESCO<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>) taxonomy provides a standardised
vocabulary of roughly 13,000 skills and competences maintained by the
European Commission. Mapping extracted skill sentences to ESCO entries
creates a common analytical framework that enables direct comparison
across countries and languages.

We employ a **custom contrastive bi-encoder** trained on a massive
synthetic dataset comprising hundreds of thousands of ESCO-labelled
examples derived from the ESCO classification structure. The
bi-encoder learns a **shared representation space** for two different
objects --- job-ad sentences and standardised ESCO skill labels --- by
bringing semantically matching pairs closer together while pushing
non-matching pairs apart. In practical terms, consider a job-ad sentence such as
*"experience with SQL and data visualisation"*. The model learns to
place it close to the correct ESCO entries --- *"use query languages"*
and *"manage databases"* --- and away from everything else. Crucially,
the training signal is strongest for **hard negatives**: skills that
are semantically nearby but not a correct match, such as *"use
programming languages"* or *"develop software"*. These are more
informative than trivially distant skills like *"negotiate contracts"*,
because the model must learn fine-grained distinctions within the same
domain rather than merely separating unrelated concepts. During
training, correct sentence–skill pairings act as positives while all
other skills in the same batch act as negatives --- with the hardest
cases (same-domain, wrong skill) driving most of the learning signal.
This gives the model a scalable way to learn ranking behaviour across
the full ~13,000-skill taxonomy without a separate classifier for each
skill.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const w = 620, h = 370;
  const cx = w / 2, cy = h / 2;

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${w} ${h}`)
    .attr("width", w)
    .attr("style", "font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; max-width: 100%; display: block; margin: 0 auto;");

  /* concentric zones */
  const zones = [
    {r: 155, fill: "#fef2f2", stroke: "#fca5a5", label: "Easy negatives", labelY: -148, col: "#dc2626"},
    {r: 105, fill: "#fefce8", stroke: "#fde68a", label: "Hard negatives", labelY: -98, col: "#b45309"},
    {r: 52,  fill: "#dcfce7", stroke: "#86efac", label: "Positive matches", labelY: -44, col: "#16a34a"}
  ];

  zones.forEach(z => {
    svg.append("circle")
      .attr("cx", cx).attr("cy", cy).attr("r", z.r)
      .attr("fill", z.fill).attr("stroke", z.stroke)
      .attr("stroke-width", 1.5).attr("stroke-dasharray", "5,3");
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", cx).attr("y", cy + z.labelY)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
      .attr("font-size", "9.5px").attr("font-weight", "600")
      .attr("fill", z.col)
      .text(z.label);
  });

  /* centre: the sentence */
  svg.append("circle")
    .attr("cx", cx).attr("cy", cy).attr("r", 6)
    .attr("fill", "#1e40af");
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", cx).attr("y", cy + 18)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", "10.5px").attr("font-weight", "700")
    .attr("fill", "#1e293b")
    .text("\"experience with SQL");
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", cx).attr("y", cy + 30)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", "10.5px").attr("font-weight", "700")
    .attr("fill", "#1e293b")
    .text("and data visualisation\"");

  /* skill dots */
  const items = [
    /* positives */
    {x: cx-28, y: cy-26, label: "use query languages", tier: "pos"},
    {x: cx+22, y: cy-18, label: "manage databases", tier: "pos"},
    /* hard negatives */
    {x: cx-80, y: cy-50, label: "use programming languages", tier: "hard"},
    {x: cx+65, y: cy+55, label: "develop software", tier: "hard"},
    {x: cx+80, y: cy-62, label: "analyse data", tier: "hard"},
    /* easy negatives */
    {x: cx-120, y: cy+95, label: "negotiate contracts", tier: "easy"},
    {x: cx+115, y: cy+110, label: "manage human resources", tier: "easy"},
    {x: cx-100, y: cy-120, label: "drive vehicles", tier: "easy"},
  ];

  const colors = {pos: "#16a34a", hard: "#b45309", easy: "#dc2626"};
  const radii  = {pos: 5, hard: 4.5, easy: 4};

  items.forEach(d => {
    svg.append("circle")
      .attr("cx", d.x).attr("cy", d.y).attr("r", radii[d.tier])
      .attr("fill", colors[d.tier]).attr("opacity", 0.85);
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", d.x).attr("y", d.y - 9)
      .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
      .attr("font-size", "9.5px").attr("font-style", "italic")
      .attr("fill", "#334155")
      .text(d.label);
  });

  /* arrows */
  svg.append("defs").append("marker")
    .attr("id", "arrowGreen").attr("viewBox", "0 0 10 10")
    .attr("refX", 8).attr("refY", 5)
    .attr("markerWidth", 6).attr("markerHeight", 6)
    .attr("orient", "auto-start-reverse")
    .append("path").attr("d", "M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z").attr("fill", "#16a34a");

  /* pull arrows from dots toward centre */
  const posItems = items.filter(d => d.tier === "pos");
  posItems.forEach(d => {
    const dx = cx - d.x, dy = cy - d.y;
    const len = Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy);
    const nx = dx/len, ny = dy/len;
    svg.append("line")
      .attr("x1", d.x + nx*8).attr("y1", d.y + ny*8)
      .attr("x2", d.x + nx*(len*0.5)).attr("y2", d.y + ny*(len*0.5))
      .attr("stroke", "#16a34a").attr("stroke-width", 1.5)
      .attr("marker-end", "url(#arrowGreen)")
      .attr("opacity", 0.7);
  });

  /* caption */
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", cx).attr("y", h - 8)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
    .attr("font-size", "10.5px").attr("fill", "#64748b")
    .text("Simplified 2-D projection of the embedding space (actual representations are high-dimensional). Own elaboration.");

  return svg.node();
}
```

This approach is well suited to online job ads because employers often
describe the same skill in many different ways. By learning semantic
proximity rather than relying on exact wording, the model can recover
skills even when the phrasing differs from the ESCO label itself.

Our approach also avoids relying only on one averaged sentence
representation. Instead, the encoder can attend more strongly to the
parts of a sentence that are most diagnostic for a given skill, which
is particularly useful when a single sentence mentions several tasks,
tools, or competencies at once. This token-level attention mechanism
has been shown to improve matching quality in multi-skill sentences
(<a class="paper-link" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.09119" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Sun, 2026<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>;
<a class="paper-link" href="https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11074185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Decorte et al., 2025<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>).

**Similarity threshold calibration.** &ensp; Not every high-ranking
candidate is a true match. Following established practice in ESCO
skill extraction
(<a class="paper-link" href="https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11074185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Decorte et al., 2025<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>;
<a class="paper-link" href="https://recsyshr.aau.dk/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/RecSysHR2025-paper_5.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Bielinski &amp; Brazier, 2025<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>),
we retain only those candidates whose cosine similarity exceeds a
calibrated threshold. The threshold is tuned on held-out benchmark
data (the same HOUSE and TECH datasets used to evaluate overall
pipeline performance) by selecting the value that maximises the
trade-off between precision and recall. This step filters out
low-confidence matches and controls the total number of skills
assigned per posting.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Our skill-mapping pipeline outperforms the current state of the art by
approximately 3 percentage points in RP@5 on the HOUSE and TECH
benchmark datasets reported by <a class="paper-link" href="https://arxiv.org/html/2505.24640v1" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Decorte et al. (2025)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span><span class="paper-tooltip" role="tooltip">Decorte, J.-J., Van Hautte, J., Develder, C., &amp; Demeester, T. (2025). <em>Efficient Text Encoders for Labor Market Analysis.</em> <em>IEEE Access</em>, 13, 133596&#8211;133608.</span></a>. These benchmarks consist of job postings annotated with ESCO gold-standard skill labels by human experts, against which model predictions are evaluated. The reported RP@5 scores are 0.822 and 0.838, respectively.
:::

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Skill-to-ESCO matching pipeline"
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'htmlLabels': true, 'nodeSpacing': 50, 'rankSpacing': 55}, 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '13px'}} }%%
flowchart TD
    A["Skill-relevant sentences"] -->|"encode"| B["Contrastive bi-encoder"]
    B -->|"768-d vector"| C["Sentence embedding"]

    D[("ESCO skill catalogue<br/>~13 000 entries")] -->|"pre-computed<br/>768-d vectors"| E["Reference embedding matrix"]

    C --> F{"Cosine similarity ranking"}
    E --> F

    F -->|"top-k candidates"| G["Candidate ESCO skills<br/>with similarity scores"]
    G -->|"score ≥ threshold"| H["Similarity threshold<br/><i style='font-size:0.8em;color:#64748b'>calibrated on held-out benchmarks</i>"]
    H --> I["Final matched ESCO skills"]
```
::::

:::: {#pipeline-step-05 .step-detail}
#### [Step 05]{.step-badge .step-badge-indigo} Job title standardisation

Raw job titles are highly variable: the same role may appear as
"Software Engineer", "Backend Developer", "Platform Engineer",
"Full-Stack Application Developer", or "Senior API Engineer". Likewise,
an analytical role may appear as "Data Analyst", "BI Analyst",
"Reporting Specialist", or "Insights Associate". Because titles are
first translated into English, the standardisation step works on a
harmonised title field rather than directly on multilingual raw text.
Mapping each title to a standardised ESCO occupation enables comparable
occupational analysis and serves as the bridge to sector classification
in the next step.

Titles are first cleaned by removing common noise (embedded locations,
salary ranges, seniority prefixes). The cleaned titles are then
embedded using
<a class="paper-link" href="https://huggingface.co/TechWolf/JobBERT-v3" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">JobBERT-v3<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>,
a domain-specific open model pre-trained on large-scale job market
data. We pre-compute embeddings for all ~3,000 ESCO occupation labels
and store them in a <a class="paper-link" href="https://github.com/facebookresearch/faiss" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">FAISS<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>
vector index for fast retrieval. Each job title is matched to the
nearest ESCO occupation by cosine similarity, with the similarity
score retained for downstream quality filtering.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Job title standardisation"
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'htmlLabels': true, 'nodeSpacing': 50, 'rankSpacing': 55}, 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '13px'}} }%%
flowchart TD
    A["Translated job title"] --> B["Title cleaning<br/><i style='font-size:0.8em;color:#64748b'>(remove locations, salaries, prefixes)</i>"]
    B -->|"cleaned text"| C["JobBERT-v3 encoder"]
    C -->|"768-d vector"| D["Title embedding"]

    D -->|"query"| F{"FAISS nearest-neighbour search"}

    E[("ESCO occupation index<br/>~3 000 occupations<br/><i style='font-size:0.8em;color:#64748b'>pre-computed 768-d vectors</i>")] -->|"index"| F

    F -->|"best match + cosine score"| G["Standardised ESCO occupation"]
```
::::

:::: {#pipeline-step-06 .step-detail}
#### [Step 06]{.step-badge .step-badge-green} Sector classification (ISIC/NACE)

Assigning an economic sector to each job posting enables analysis of
skill demand by industry---a dimension that is critical for targeted
workforce development and industrial policy. We classify postings into
the **ISIC/NACE** industry taxonomy. The underlying crosswalk uses the
full NACE Rev. 2.1 classification maintained by Eurostat, which provides
finer sub-division detail (3- and 4-digit codes) than ISIC. However,
because this report analyses sectoral demand at the **2-digit division
level**, the results are identical under both systems: at 2 digits,
NACE Rev. 2.1 and ISIC Rev. 5 share the same division codes and
boundaries
(<a class="paper-link" href="https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/documents/3859598/21633320/KS-GQ-24-007-EN-N.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Eurostat, 2024<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>).
All results in this report are therefore directly comparable with any
analysis using either framework.

The assignment relies on the official
<a class="paper-link" href="https://esco.ec.europa.eu/en/about-esco/escopedia/escopedia/nace" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ESCO-to-NACE crosswalk<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>,
which links each ESCO occupation to one or more ISIC/NACE activity
codes. Three resolution tiers handle different levels of ambiguity:

1. **One-to-one mapping** — the ESCO occupation maps to a single
   ISIC/NACE code. The assignment is deterministic.
2. **Same 2-digit code** — all candidate codes share the same 2-digit
   division (e.g. all fall under *C26*). The shared division is assigned
   directly, since finer sub-division differences are below the
   reporting threshold.
3. **Ambiguous multi-division cases** — candidates span different
   ISIC/NACE divisions. We resolve these by embedding the job title and
   description together with each candidate activity label using a
   multilingual sentence-transformer
   (<a class="paper-link" href="https://huggingface.co/sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>)
   and selecting the candidate with the highest cosine similarity. A
   confidence margin between the top two candidates is recorded to
   support post-hoc quality auditing.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "ISIC/NACE sector classification"
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'htmlLabels': true, 'nodeSpacing': 50, 'rankSpacing': 55}, 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '13px'}} }%%
flowchart TD
    A["ESCO occupation<br/><i style='font-size:0.8em;color:#64748b'>from Step 05</i>"] -->|"look up"| B[("ESCO → ISIC/NACE crosswalk<br/><i style='font-size:0.8em;color:#64748b'>official mapping table</i>")]
    B --> C{"Number of<br/>candidate ISIC/NACE codes?"}

    C -->|"exactly 1"| D["Deterministic assignment"]
    C -->|"≥ 2, same 2-digit code"| E["Assign shared<br/>ISIC/NACE division"]
    C -->|"≥ 2, different divisions"| F["Embedding-based disambiguation"]

    F --> G["Encode job text + each<br/>candidate ISIC/NACE label"]
    G -->|"384-d vectors"| H{"Cosine similarity comparison"}
    H -->|"highest score"| I["Assigned ISIC/NACE code<br/>+ confidence margin"]

    D --> J["Final sector label"]
    E --> J
    I --> J
```
::::

:::


## Results

### Skill content of job postings

::: {.section-card}
#### Skills per offer

The number of identifiable skills per posting varies substantially
across countries, reflecting differences not only in underlying labour
demand but also in how online vacancies are written and used. In some
markets, postings resemble detailed job descriptions and explicitly
list technical, language, and transversal requirements, often in more
standardized HR formats; in others, they are shorter and function
primarily as vacancy notices, with much of the screening and
clarification taking place later in the recruitment process.
Cross-country differences may also partly reflect variation in the
occupational composition of online demand, as professional and
technical roles tend to generate more skill-rich vacancy texts than
more generic or less formal postings. The indicator should therefore
be interpreted primarily as capturing the explicitness and
codification of skill demand in online recruitment, rather than as a
direct measure of job complexity across countries.

The table below reports the distribution of ESCO-mapped skills per
posting. Click a column header to sort.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const rows = [
    { country: "India", mean: 7.6, p10: 1, p25: 3, median: 6, p75: 11, p90: 16 },
    { country: "Indonesia", mean: 5.5, p10: 1, p25: 1, median: 3, p75: 8, p90: 14 },
    { country: "Japan", mean: 5.5, p10: 1, p25: 2, median: 5, p75: 8, p90: 11 },
    { country: "S. Korea", mean: 3.3, p10: 0, p25: 1, median: 2, p75: 4, p90: 9 },
    { country: "Malaysia", mean: 7.9, p10: 1, p25: 2, median: 7, p75: 12, p90: 17 },
    { country: "Mexico", mean: 6.3, p10: 1, p25: 3, median: 5, p75: 9, p90: 13 },
    { country: "Philippines", mean: 8.2, p10: 1, p25: 3, median: 7, p75: 11, p90: 16 },
    { country: "Poland", mean: 10.0, p10: 3, p25: 6, median: 10, p75: 13, p90: 17 },
    { country: "Singapore", mean: 9.0, p10: 2, p25: 5, median: 8, p75: 12, p90: 17 },
    { country: "Thailand", mean: 5.8, p10: 1, p25: 1, median: 4, p75: 9, p90: 14 },
    { country: "Taiwan", mean: 8.7, p10: 0, p25: 2, median: 7, p75: 12, p90: 20 },
    { country: "Vietnam", mean: 9.7, p10: 3, p25: 6, median: 9, p75: 13, p90: 17 },
    { country: "Overall", mean: 7.2, p10: 1, p25: 2, median: 6, p75: 10, p90: 15, overall: true },
  ];

  const table = d3.create("table")
    .attr("class", "table table-sm align-middle market-table");

  const columns = [
    { key: "country", label: "Country", align: "left", type: "text" },
    { key: "mean", label: "Mean", align: "right", type: "number" },
    { key: "p25", label: "P25", align: "right", type: "number" },
    { key: "median", label: "Median", align: "right", type: "number" },
    { key: "p75", label: "P75", align: "right", type: "number" },
    { key: "p90", label: "P90", align: "right", type: "number" },
  ];

  let sortKey = "mean";
  let sortDirection = "desc";

  const thead = table.append("thead");
  const header = thead.append("tr");
  const tbody = table.append("tbody");

  const sortRows = () => {
    const baseRows = rows.filter(d => !d.overall);
    const overall = rows.find(d => d.overall);
    const sorted = [...baseRows].sort((a, b) => {
      const av = a[sortKey];
      const bv = b[sortKey];
      const cmp = columns.find(c => c.key === sortKey).type === "text"
        ? String(av).localeCompare(String(bv))
        : av - bv;
      return sortDirection === "asc" ? cmp : -cmp;
    });
    return overall ? [...sorted, overall] : sorted;
  };

  const renderBody = () => {
    tbody.selectAll("tr").remove();
    sortRows().forEach(row => {
      const tr = tbody.append("tr")
        .style("font-weight", row.overall ? "700" : null)
        .style("background", row.overall ? "#f8fafc" : null);
      tr.append("td").text(row.country);
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").style("font-variant-numeric", "tabular-nums").text(fmtRate(row.mean));
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").style("font-variant-numeric", "tabular-nums").text(fmtRate(row.p25));
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").style("font-variant-numeric", "tabular-nums").text(fmtRate(row.median));
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").style("font-variant-numeric", "tabular-nums").text(fmtRate(row.p75));
      tr.append("td").style("text-align", "right").style("font-variant-numeric", "tabular-nums").text(fmtRate(row.p90));
    });
  };

  columns.forEach(col => header.append("th")
    .attr("class", col.key === sortKey ? "active-sort" : null)
    .style("text-align", col.align)
    .style("font-variant-numeric", col.align === "right" ? "tabular-nums" : null)
    .text(`${col.label}${col.key === sortKey ? (sortDirection === "asc" ? " ↑" : " ↓") : ""}`)
    .on("click", function() {
      if (sortKey === col.key) {
        sortDirection = sortDirection === "asc" ? "desc" : "asc";
      } else {
        sortKey = col.key;
        sortDirection = col.type === "text" ? "asc" : "desc";
      }
      header.selectAll("th")
        .attr("class", null)
        .text((d, i) => {
          const c = columns[i];
          return `${c.label}${c.key === sortKey ? (sortDirection === "asc" ? " ↑" : " ↓") : ""}`;
        });
      header.selectAll("th")
        .filter((d, i) => columns[i].key === sortKey)
        .attr("class", "active-sort");
      renderBody();
    }));

  renderBody();

  return table.node();
}
```
:::

### Sectoral demand structure (ISIC/NACE)

::: {.section-card}
#### Sectoral heatmap

Each job posting is assigned an economic sector via the ISIC/NACE
classification derived from the ESCO occupation mapping (see
[Step 06](#pipeline-step-06) above). At the 2-digit division level used
in this analysis, NACE Rev. 2.1 and ISIC Rev. 5 are identical. The
heatmap below shows the share of postings in each ISIC/NACE section
(1-digit) per country. Clicking on any section row expands a 2-digit
breakdown showing how demand is distributed across divisions within
that sector.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

nace2d = FileAttachment("nace_2digit_detail.json").json()

{
  const eaList = ["JP","KR","TW","TH","MY","SG","ID","VN","PH"];
  const bmList = ["IN","MX","PL"];
  const countryFull = {JP:"Japan",KR:"S. Korea",TW:"Taiwan",TH:"Thailand",MY:"Malaysia",SG:"Singapore",ID:"Indonesia",VN:"Vietnam",PH:"Philippines",IN:"India",MX:"Mexico",PL:"Poland"};
  const sections = [
    {l:"N",n:"Professional, scientific & technical"},
    {l:"G",n:"Wholesale & retail trade"},
    {l:"O",n:"Administrative & support"},
    {l:"K",n:"Telecom, IT & information services"},
    {l:"I",n:"Accommodation & food service"},
    {l:"C",n:"Manufacturing"},
    {l:"H",n:"Transportation & storage"},
    {l:"R",n:"Human health & social work"},
    {l:"Q",n:"Education"},
    {l:"S",n:"Arts, sports & recreation"},
    {l:"L",n:"Financial & insurance"},
    {l:"T",n:"Other service activities"},
    {l:"F",n:"Construction"},
    {l:"P",n:"Public administration & defence"},
    {l:"U",n:"Households as employers"},
    {l:"J",n:"Publishing & broadcasting"},
    {l:"M",n:"Real estate"},
    {l:"A",n:"Agriculture & fishing"},
    {l:"D",n:"Electricity & gas"},
    {l:"E",n:"Water supply & waste"},
    {l:"B",n:"Mining & quarrying"}
  ];
  const pct = {
    N:{ID:28.2,IN:26.2,JP:8.4,KR:13.1,MY:25.2,MX:17.5,PH:27.8,PL:20.5,SG:32.8,TH:20.1,TW:15.3,VN:27.6},
    G:{ID:13.0,IN:8.4,JP:9.2,KR:7.1,MY:11.7,MX:16.3,PH:13.3,PL:18.5,SG:7.8,TH:13.4,TW:16.1,VN:17.0},
    O:{ID:9.7,IN:14.0,JP:11.4,KR:10.3,MY:13.4,MX:15.2,PH:16.2,PL:9.1,SG:8.7,TH:8.9,TW:9.9,VN:10.9},
    K:{ID:6.9,IN:11.4,JP:9.8,KR:2.7,MY:4.4,MX:3.0,PH:4.9,PL:12.0,SG:10.3,TH:2.7,TW:2.5,VN:10.2},
    I:{ID:13.6,IN:5.0,JP:8.1,KR:4.5,MY:14.2,MX:5.6,PH:5.4,PL:1.5,SG:7.4,TH:24.4,TW:9.4,VN:4.7},
    C:{ID:4.3,IN:4.4,JP:9.8,KR:14.5,MY:5.0,MX:7.8,PH:5.1,PL:8.5,SG:5.1,TH:4.4,TW:12.8,VN:5.5},
    H:{ID:4.5,IN:2.4,JP:9.6,KR:4.4,MY:5.6,MX:10.1,PH:6.6,PL:6.3,SG:5.1,TH:9.1,TW:6.1,VN:3.5},
    R:{ID:1.2,IN:4.6,JP:9.3,KR:13.5,MY:2.8,MX:3.1,PH:3.0,PL:4.3,SG:2.8,TH:1.0,TW:4.2,VN:1.2},
    Q:{ID:3.5,IN:7.6,JP:4.1,KR:6.4,MY:2.9,MX:2.5,PH:2.1,PL:2.1,SG:4.3,TH:2.0,TW:3.6,VN:2.8},
    S:{ID:3.4,IN:2.1,JP:3.0,KR:2.5,MY:3.6,MX:2.6,PH:2.2,PL:1.9,SG:2.3,TH:3.6,TW:3.5,VN:1.9},
    L:{ID:2.4,IN:3.3,JP:0.5,KR:1.0,MY:1.5,MX:6.1,PH:3.1,PL:3.5,SG:3.7,TH:1.2,TW:1.4,VN:4.5},
    T:{ID:2.5,IN:1.8,JP:2.4,KR:1.9,MY:2.5,MX:3.6,PH:2.1,PL:3.4,SG:2.5,TH:2.9,TW:2.3,VN:1.8},
    F:{ID:0.7,IN:0.9,JP:4.2,KR:2.5,MY:1.0,MX:1.6,PH:1.0,PL:2.1,SG:1.7,TH:0.9,TW:3.6,VN:1.3},
    P:{ID:2.5,IN:2.1,JP:3.5,KR:2.5,MY:1.9,MX:1.6,PH:2.5,PL:2.2,SG:2.4,TH:2.6,TW:3.0,VN:2.3},
    U:{ID:0.1,IN:0.2,JP:3.0,KR:8.2,MY:0.4,MX:0.3,PH:0.4,PL:0.4,SG:0.1,TH:0.5,TW:0.6,VN:0.2},
    J:{ID:1.2,IN:3.2,JP:0.8,KR:1.1,MY:1.9,MX:0.4,PH:1.1,PL:1.4,SG:1.1,TH:0.7,TW:1.9,VN:2.4},
    M:{ID:0.7,IN:1.1,JP:0.9,KR:0.8,MY:0.6,MX:0.8,PH:1.2,PL:0.6,SG:0.9,TH:0.5,TW:0.9,VN:0.9},
    A:{ID:0.7,IN:0.4,JP:0.6,KR:1.6,MY:0.7,MX:0.7,PH:0.9,PL:0.7,SG:0.4,TH:0.5,TW:1.4,VN:1.0},
    D:{ID:0.3,IN:0.5,JP:0.4,KR:0.5,MY:0.3,MX:0.5,PH:0.6,PL:0.5,SG:0.3,TH:0.3,TW:0.6,VN:0.2},
    E:{ID:0.2,IN:0.1,JP:0.7,KR:0.7,MY:0.2,MX:0.4,PH:0.3,PL:0.3,SG:0.1,TH:0.2,TW:0.4,VN:0.2},
    B:{ID:0.2,IN:0.3,JP:0.4,KR:0.3,MY:0.2,MX:0.3,PH:0.1,PL:0.2,SG:0.2,TH:0.2,TW:0.6,VN:0.1}
  };
  const detail = nace2d;

  const color = d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateYlOrRd).domain([0, 33]);
  const subColor = d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateBlues).domain([0, 100]);

  const wrap = d3.create("div")
    .style("font-family","system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif")
    .style("font-size","12px");

  /* ── toggle buttons ── */
  const btnBar = wrap.append("div")
    .style("display","flex").style("gap","0").style("margin-bottom","12px");

  const btnStyle = (active) =>
    `padding:6px 18px;font-size:12px;font-weight:${active?700:500};cursor:pointer;border:1px solid #cbd5e1;`
    + `background:${active?"#0f172a":"#fff"};color:${active?"#fff":"#475569"};`
    + `transition:all .15s`;

  const btnEA = btnBar.append("button")
    .attr("style", btnStyle(true) + ";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px")
    .text("East Asia");
  const btnBM = btnBar.append("button")
    .attr("style", btnStyle(false) + ";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none")
    .text("Benchmarks");

  /* ── table host ── */
  const tableHost = wrap.append("div");
  const legendHost = wrap.append("div")
    .style("margin-top","10px").style("font-size","11px")
    .style("color","#64748b").style("line-height","1.6");

  function shortName(name, n) {
    return name.length > n ? name.slice(0, n - 1) + "\u2026" : name;
  }

  function cellBg(val, scale) {
    const bg = scale(val);
    return `background:${bg};color:${val > 18 ? "#fff" : "#1e293b"}`;
  }

  function buildTable(countries) {
    tableHost.html("");
    legendHost.html("");

    const tbl = tableHost.append("table")
```
