```{ojs}
      .style("border-collapse","collapse").style("width","auto");

    /* header */
    const ch = tbl.append("thead").append("tr");
    ch.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","2px 6px")
      .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8").text("ISIC/NACE section");
    countries.forEach(c => {
      ch.append("th").style("padding","2px 6px").style("text-align","center")
        .style("font-size","11px").style("font-weight","700")
        .style("color","#334155").style("min-width","48px").text(c);
    });

    const tbody = tbl.append("tbody");

    sections.forEach(s => {
      const divs = detail[s.l] || [];
      const hasDivs = divs.length > 1;

      const tr = tbody.append("tr").style("cursor", hasDivs ? "pointer" : "default");
      const labelTd = tr.append("td")
        .style("text-align","right").style("padding","3px 6px")
        .style("white-space","nowrap").style("font-size","11px")
        .style("color","#475569");
      labelTd.html(`${hasDivs ? '<span class="nace-arrow" style="display:inline-block;width:14px;font-size:9px;transition:transform .2s">\u25B6</span>' : '<span style="display:inline-block;width:14px"></span>'}${s.l}\u2002${s.n}`);

      countries.forEach(c => {
        const val = pct[s.l][c];
        tr.append("td")
          .attr("style", `text-align:center;padding:2px 6px;border-radius:3px;font-size:10px;font-weight:${val >= 10 ? 700 : 400};${cellBg(val, color)}`)
          .text(val.toFixed(1));
      });

      const subRows = [];
      if (hasDivs) {
        divs.forEach(d => {
          const sr = tbody.append("tr").style("display","none");
          sr.append("td")
            .style("text-align","right").style("padding","2px 6px 2px 24px")
            .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8")
            .style("white-space","nowrap")
            .text(`${d.div} ${shortName(d.name, 38)}`);
          countries.forEach(c => {
            const val = d.pct[c] || 0;
            sr.append("td")
              .attr("style", `text-align:center;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:3px;font-size:9px;${cellBg(val, subColor)}`)
              .text(val.toFixed(0));
          });
          subRows.push(sr);
        });

        let open = false;
        tr.on("click", () => {
          open = !open;
          labelTd.select(".nace-arrow").style("transform", open ? "rotate(90deg)" : "rotate(0deg)");
          subRows.forEach(sr => sr.style("display", open ? "table-row" : "none"));
        });
      }
    });

    /* legend */
    const items = countries.map(c =>
      `<span style="font-weight:700">${c}</span>\u2009${countryFull[c]}`
    );
    legendHost.html(items.join(" \u00b7 "));
  }

  /* initial render */
  buildTable(eaList);

  btnEA.on("click", () => {
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true) + ";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false) + ";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    buildTable(eaList);
  });
  btnBM.on("click", () => {
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false) + ";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true) + ";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    buildTable(bmList);
  });

  return wrap.node();
}
```

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
##### Statistical test: do countries differ in sectoral composition?

A global $\chi^2$ test of independence on the 12×21 contingency table
(country × ISIC/NACE section) yields $\chi^2 = 393{,}255$, $df = 220$,
$p < 0.001$, confirming that sectoral demand structures differ
significantly across the 12 countries. The overall effect size is
Cramér's $V = 0.14$, indicating a moderate but meaningful departure
from homogeneity.

**Pairwise similarity** (Cramér's $V$, lower = more similar):

| Most similar pairs | $V$  | Most different pairs | $V$  |
|-----|------|------|------|
| Indonesia – Malaysia    | 0.12 | S. Korea – Vietnam   | 0.46 |
| Malaysia – Thailand     | 0.17 | Indonesia – S. Korea | 0.44 |
| India – Singapore       | 0.17 | S. Korea – Thailand  | 0.44 |
| Indonesia – Singapore   | 0.17 | S. Korea – Singapore | 0.43 |
:::

#### Macroeconomic and demographic context

The tables below provide a reference frame for interpreting the sectoral
demand patterns. All data are from the
<a class="paper-link" href="https://data.worldbank.org/indicator" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">World Bank Open Data<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>
(latest available year, 2023–2024), except Taiwan, for which data are
sourced from the National Statistics Bureau and the National Development
Council.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

macroCtx = FileAttachment("macro_context.json").json()

{
  const eaList = ["JP","KR","TW","TH","MY","SG","ID","VN","PH"];
  const bmList = ["IN","MX","PL"];
  const countryFull = {JP:"Japan",KR:"S. Korea",TW:"Taiwan",TH:"Thailand",
    MY:"Malaysia",SG:"Singapore",ID:"Indonesia",VN:"Vietnam",
    PH:"Philippines",IN:"India",MX:"Mexico",PL:"Poland"};
  const d = macroCtx;

  const wrap = d3.create("div")
    .style("font-family","system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif")
    .style("font-size","12px");

  /* ── toggle ── */
  const btnBar = wrap.append("div").style("display","flex").style("gap","0")
    .style("margin-bottom","12px");
  const btnStyle = (active) =>
    `padding:6px 18px;font-size:12px;font-weight:${active?700:500};`
    + `cursor:pointer;border:1px solid #cbd5e1;`
    + `background:${active?"#0f172a":"#fff"};color:${active?"#fff":"#475569"};`
    + `transition:all .15s`;
  const btnEA = btnBar.append("button")
    .attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px")
    .text("East Asia");
  const btnBM = btnBar.append("button")
    .attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none")
    .text("Benchmarks");

  const host = wrap.append("div");

  /* ── helpers ── */
  function fmt(v,dec) {
    return v!=null ? v.toLocaleString("en",{minimumFractionDigits:dec,
      maximumFractionDigits:dec}) : "\u2014";
  }
  function fmtK(v) {
    return v!=null ? v.toLocaleString("en",{maximumFractionDigits:0}) : "\u2014";
  }

  const incomeOrder = {"High":3,"Upper-middle":2,"Lower-middle":1,"Low":0};
  const incomeColor = {"High":"#16a34a","Upper-middle":"#3b82f6",
    "Lower-middle":"#f59e0b"};

  /* ── generic sortable table builder ── */
  function sortableTable(parent, title, cols, rows, defaultSortCol,
                         defaultAsc, sourceHtml) {
    parent.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","12px")
      .style("margin","18px 0 6px").style("color","#0f172a").text(title);

    const tblWrap = parent.append("div");
    let sortCol = defaultSortCol;
    let sortAsc = defaultAsc;

    function render() {
      tblWrap.html("");
      const sorted = [...rows].sort((a,b) => {
        const va = a.vals[sortCol], vb = b.vals[sortCol];
        if (va == null && vb == null) return 0;
        if (va == null) return 1;
        if (vb == null) return -1;
        const cmp = typeof va === "string"
          ? va.localeCompare(vb) : va - vb;
        return sortAsc ? cmp : -cmp;
      });

      const tbl = tblWrap.append("table")
        .style("border-collapse","collapse").style("margin-bottom","4px");
      const thead = tbl.append("thead").append("tr");
      cols.forEach((c,i) => {
        const arrow = sortCol === i
          ? (sortAsc ? " \u25B2" : " \u25BC") : "";
        thead.append("th")
          .style("text-align", c.align || "right")
          .style("padding","4px 10px")
          .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8")
          .style("font-weight","600")
          .style("border-bottom","1px solid #e2e8f0")
          .style("white-space","nowrap")
          .style("cursor","pointer")
          .style("user-select","none")
          .text(c.label + arrow)
          .on("click", () => {
            if (sortCol === i) sortAsc = !sortAsc;
            else { sortCol = i; sortAsc = c.defaultAsc ?? false; }
            render();
          });
      });

      sorted.forEach(row => {
        const tr = tbl.append("tbody").append("tr");
        cols.forEach((c,i) => {
          const v = row.vals[i];
          const td = tr.append("td")
            .style("padding","3px 10px")
            .style("text-align", c.align || "right")
            .style("font-size","11px")
            .style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums");
          if (c.render) c.render(td, v, row);
          else td.text(v != null ? v : "\u2014");
        });
      });

      if (sourceHtml) {
        tblWrap.append("div").style("font-size","9px").style("color","#b0b8c4")
          .style("margin-top","2px").style("line-height","1.4").html(sourceHtml);
      }
    }
    render();
  }

  /* ── build all tables ── */
  function build(countries) {
    host.html("");

    /* ── Table 1: Demographics ── */
    const rows1 = countries.map(cc => {
      const r = d[cc]||{};
      return {cc, vals: [
        countryFull[cc],
        r.income_group || "",
        r.gdp_cap_ppp,
        r.old_age_dep,
        r.fertility
      ]};
    });
    const cols1 = [
      {label:"Country", align:"left", defaultAsc:true,
        render:(td,v)=>td.style("font-weight","600").text(v)},
      {label:"Income group", align:"left", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v,row)=>{
          td.style("font-size","10px").style("font-weight","600")
            .style("color",incomeColor[v]||"#475569").text(v);
          row.vals[1] = incomeOrder[v]||0;
        }},
      {label:"GDP/cap PPP ($)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmtK(v))},
      {label:"Old-age dep. ratio", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>{
          const c = v>=25?"#dc2626":v>=15?"#f59e0b":"#475569";
          td.style("color",c).style("font-weight",v>=25?"700":"400")
            .text(fmt(v,1));
        }},
      {label:"Fertility rate", defaultAsc:true,
        render:(td,v)=>{
          const c = v<1.3?"#dc2626":v<1.8?"#f59e0b":"#475569";
          td.style("color",c).style("font-weight",v<1.3?"700":"400")
            .text(fmt(v,2));
        }}
    ];
    sortableTable(host, "Demographic structure and income level",
      cols1, rows1, 3, false,
      "WDI: <code>NY.GDP.PCAP.PP.CD</code>, <code>SP.POP.DPND.OL</code>, "
      + "<code>SP.DYN.TFRT.IN</code>. "
      + "Taiwan: DGBAS National Accounts &amp; MOI Demographic Statistics.");

    /* ── Table 2: FDI ── */
    const rows2 = countries.map(cc => {
      const r = d[cc]||{};
      return {cc, vals: [
        countryFull[cc],
        r.fdi_pct_2024,
        r.fdi_pct_avg,
        r.fdi_bn_2024,
        r.fdi_bn_avg
      ]};
    });
    const barRender = (td,v) => {
      td.style("min-width","90px");
      td.append("div").style("text-align","right").style("font-size","11px")
        .style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums").text(fmt(v,1));
      const barW = Math.min((v||0)/30*100,100);
      td.append("div").style("width","100%").style("height","4px")
        .style("background","#e2e8f0").style("border-radius","2px")
        .style("margin-top","2px")
        .append("div").style("width",barW+"%").style("height","100%")
        .style("background","#3b82f6").style("border-radius","2px")
        .style("min-width", v>0?"2px":"0");
    };
    const cols2 = [
      {label:"Country", align:"left", defaultAsc:true,
        render:(td,v)=>td.style("font-weight","600").text(v)},
      {label:"FDI % GDP (2024)", defaultAsc:false, render:barRender},
      {label:"FDI % GDP (5-yr avg)", defaultAsc:false, render:barRender},
      {label:"FDI US$ bn (2024)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmt(v,1))},
      {label:"FDI US$ bn (5-yr avg)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmt(v,1))}
    ];
    sortableTable(host, "Foreign direct investment, net inflows",
      cols2, rows2, 1, false,
      "WDI: <code>BX.KLT.DINV.WD.GD.ZS</code>, "
      + "<code>BX.KLT.DINV.CD.WD</code>. 5-yr avg = 2020\u20132024. "
      + "Taiwan: Investment Commission, MOEA. "
      + "Singapore FDI/GDP reflects its financial-hub role "
      + "(gross flows exceed GDP).");

    /* ── Table 3: Economic structure ── */
    const rows3 = countries.map(cc => {
      const r = d[cc]||{};
      return {cc, vals: [
        countryFull[cc],
        r.manuf_pct,
        r.services_pct,
        r.exports_pct
      ]};
    });
    const cols3 = [
      {label:"Country", align:"left", defaultAsc:true,
        render:(td,v)=>td.style("font-weight","600").text(v)},
      {label:"Manufacturing (% GDP)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmt(v,1))},
      {label:"Services (% GDP)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmt(v,1))},
      {label:"Exports (% GDP)", defaultAsc:false,
        render:(td,v)=>td.text(fmt(v,1))}
    ];
    sortableTable(host, "Economic structure",
      cols3, rows3, 1, false,
      "WDI: <code>NV.IND.MANF.ZS</code>, <code>NV.SRV.TOTL.ZS</code>, "
      + "<code>NE.EXP.GNFS.ZS</code>. "
      + "Taiwan: DGBAS National Accounts.");

    /* ── definitions ── */
    host.append("div").style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8")
      .style("margin-top","12px").style("line-height","1.5")
      .html("<b>Old-age dependency ratio</b> = number of people aged 65+ "
        + "per 100 working-age population (15\u201364); higher values "
        + "indicate a larger elderly population relative to working-age "
        + "adults (e.g.\u00a0Japan\u2019s 50.7 means roughly one senior "
        + "for every two workers). "
        + "<b>Fertility rate</b> (total, births per woman) = average "
        + "number of children born per woman over her lifetime; a rate "
        + "below ~2.1 implies the population will shrink without "
        + "net immigration (e.g.\u00a0South Korea\u2019s 0.75 is the "
        + "lowest recorded globally). Click any column header to sort.");
  }

  build(eaList);
  btnEA.on("click", () => {
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false)
      +";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    build(eaList);
  });
  btnBM.on("click", () => {
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true)
      +";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    build(bmList);
  });

  return wrap.node();
}
```

#### Sectoral demand patterns across East Asia

Several structural patterns emerge from the sectoral heatmap, which can
be read against the macroeconomic indicators above. The cross-country
variation reflects differences in income level, productive structure,
demographic trajectory, FDI exposure, and the degree of formalization of
national labour markets.

**Manufacturing (C): high-tech industrial economies overrepresent
manufacturing in online demand.** South Korea (14.5% of postings),
Taiwan (12.8%) and Japan (9.8%) display the largest manufacturing
shares. However, the relationship between manufacturing's share of GDP
and its share of online job postings is not linear. Taiwan's
manufacturing sector accounts for 36.4% of GDP---the highest in the
sample---yet only 12.8% of postings, while Thailand (24.3% of GDP) and
Vietnam (24.4%) generate only 4.4% and 5.5% of postings respectively.
The key differentiator is *what kind* of manufacturing drives demand:
Korea's and Japan's semiconductor, automotive and precision-engineering
industries generate high volumes of formal online vacancies for skilled
workers (engineers, technicians, quality specialists), whereas
manufacturing in Southeast Asia is more labour-intensive and relies
more heavily on informal recruitment channels. This is reinforced by
the pattern within ISIC/NACE section N (Professional, scientific and
technical activities): *architectural and engineering services*
(ISIC/NACE division 71) represent 38% of professional-sector postings
in Japan and 32% in Korea, compared with only 8–9% in Indonesia,
Vietnam or India. In other words, the high-tech industrial base in
Japan, Korea and Taiwan generates demand that spills over into
engineering services, amplifying these countries' industrial footprint
beyond section C alone.

**Professional and scientific activities (N): FDI composition matters
more than FDI volume.** Singapore (32.8%), Indonesia (28.2%), the
Philippines (27.8%) and Vietnam (27.6%) lead in ISIC/NACE section N,
while Japan (8.4%) and Korea (13.1%) trail far behind. The 2-digit
decomposition explains why: 54–56% of section N postings in Singapore,
Indonesia, India and the Philippines consist of *activities of head
offices and management consultancy* (ISIC/NACE division 70). This
aligns with FDI patterns---Singapore channels 24.7% of GDP through
foreign direct investment, Vietnam 4.2%, the Philippines 2.0%---and
with the presence of regional headquarters, shared-service centres and
international consulting firms in these economies. In Japan (FDI/GDP:
0.4%) and Korea (0.7%), ISIC/NACE division 70 is notably smaller
(33–34%), while engineering services (division 71) dominate the
professional sector, reflecting domestic industrial demand rather than
external corporate activity.

**Accommodation and food services (I) track tourism dependence.**
Thailand (24.4%), Malaysia (14.2%) and Indonesia (13.6%) stand out.
These are upper-middle-income economies where services account for a
large share of GDP (59.2%, 53.7% and 43.8% respectively) and tourism
is a major contributor. In high-income economies---Japan (8.1%), Korea
(4.5%), Singapore (7.4%)---the hospitality sector's share of online
postings is markedly lower relative to overall services orientation
(services represent 69.8%, 57.5% and 73.0% of GDP).

**Health and social work (R): demographic pressure meets labour market
formalization.** The most distinctive country-level finding concerns
ISIC/NACE section R. South Korea (13.5%) and Japan (9.3%) far exceed
the shares observed in Southeast Asian economies (1.0–2.8%), even
though health systems across the region face similar underlying
challenges. The 2-digit breakdown reveals that *residential care
activities* (ISIC/NACE division 87) account for 48% of section R
postings in Korea and 37% in Japan, while *social work activities
without accommodation* (ISIC/NACE division 88) adds another 27–28%.
This demand for elderly care workers, home-visit aides and social
workers is a direct reflection of population ageing: Japan's old-age
dependency ratio has reached 50.7---by far the highest in the
sample---and South Korea's fertility rate of 0.75 is the lowest in the
world. Poland (old-age dependency: 31.0) also has a relatively elevated
share in section R (4.3%), consistent with this interpretation.

However, the high online visibility of care occupations in Japan and
Korea also reflects the degree of *labour market formalization*: in
these countries, care work is overwhelmingly provided through regulated
institutions---licensed nursing homes, certified home-care agencies,
municipal social welfare offices---that post vacancies on formal job
platforms. In lower-income economies with younger populations
(Philippines: old-age dependency 8.2, Indonesia: 10.7), much of the
care economy operates informally and does not appear in online postings.
The observed gap therefore captures both a real demographic demand
effect and a measurement artefact linked to formalization.

**IT and telecommunications (K): sectoral classification, not
technological backwardness.** Poland (12.0%), India (11.4%), Singapore
(10.3%), Vietnam (10.2%) and Japan (9.8%) lead in ISIC/NACE section K,
which is almost entirely composed of *computer programming, consultancy
and related activities* (ISIC/NACE division 62; 82–91% across all
countries). The relatively low shares in Korea (2.7%) and Taiwan (2.5%)
are counterintuitive given these countries' technological
sophistication---Korea's manufacturing alone represents 26.6% of GDP
and Taiwan's 36.4%, both dominated by electronics and semiconductors.
The explanation lies in how firms organize and classify IT functions:
Korean and Taiwanese conglomerates (Samsung, TSMC, SK Hynix, LG) embed
software engineers and data scientists within manufacturing divisions,
where vacancies are classified under ISIC/NACE section C
(Manufacturing) or division N/71 (Engineering) rather than as standalone
software services (section K). India and Vietnam, by contrast, have
large IT outsourcing and offshoring sectors---serving external
clients---that are classified explicitly as software services,
amplifying section K.

**Financial services (L) reflect financial-sector maturity and
formalization dynamics.** Mexico (6.1%), Vietnam (4.5%) and Singapore
(3.7%) lead---reflecting, respectively, a large domestic banking sector
with high turnover, a rapidly formalizing financial system in a
lower-middle-income economy (GDP/cap: $16,386 PPP), and a global
financial hub. Japan (0.5%) and Korea (1.0%) record the lowest shares;
their mature financial sectors, with low turnover, generate
proportionally fewer new vacancies relative to total market size.

**Wholesale and retail trade (G) is largest in economies undergoing
retail formalization**---Poland (18.5%), Vietnam (17.0%), Mexico (16.3%),
Taiwan (16.1%)---and smallest in Korea (7.1%) and Singapore (7.8%),
where the sector has consolidated and e-commerce has reduced the
intensity of vacancy creation per unit of retail output.

In summary, the sectoral composition of online job demand reflects real
structural differences that are consistent with macroeconomic
fundamentals. High-income East Asian economies display a pronounced
high-tech manufacturing and engineering orientation---visible not only
in section C but also in the engineering-services component of section
N---alongside demographically driven care-sector demand in section R.
Southeast Asian and benchmark economies exhibit stronger service-sector
profiles shaped by tourism (section I), FDI-driven consulting and
shared-service centres (section N / division 70), and the expanding
formalization of retail and financial employment. These patterns offer
actionable signals for workforce development policy: for ageing
societies, the priority lies in scaling up care-sector training and
migration pathways; for FDI-receiving economies, it lies in upgrading
professional and managerial skills to move up the value chain from
back-office operations toward higher-value functions.

#### Online demand vs. actual employment structure

The table below contrasts the occupational composition of online job
postings (**demand**) with actual employment shares from national
labour force surveys reported to the ILO (**employment**), both at
the ISCO-08 Major Group level (1-digit).

For each country we compute the **Index of Dissimilarity**, originally
developed to measure residential segregation by race and gender. The
same functional form is widely applied in labour market mismatch
research — for instance,
<a class="paper-link" href="https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11135-023-01707-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Beręsewicz et al. (2024)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a>
uses it to quantify educational and occupational mismatch between
online job postings and employer-survey data in Poland.

$$
\text{Mismatch}_j = \frac{1}{2} \sum_{i} \left| D_{ij} - E_{ij} \right|
$$

where $D_{ij}$ is the share of online job postings in ISCO group $i$
for country $j$ and $E_{ij}$ is the corresponding employment share
from national labour force surveys. The index expresses the percentage
of the employment structure that would need to shift between
occupational groups to match the composition of online demand.

Use the country selector below to browse the detailed breakdown.
The summary bar chart ranks all countries by mismatch.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

isco1ds = FileAttachment("isco1_demand_supply.json").json()

{
  const eaList = ["JP","KR","TH","MY","SG","ID","VN","PH"];
  const bmList = ["IN","MX","PL"];
  const allList = [...eaList, ...bmList];
  const countryFull = {JP:"Japan",KR:"S. Korea",TH:"Thailand",MY:"Malaysia",SG:"Singapore",ID:"Indonesia",VN:"Vietnam",PH:"Philippines",IN:"India",MX:"Mexico",PL:"Poland"};
  const iscoNames = {
    "0":"Armed forces","1":"Managers","2":"Professionals",
    "2+3":"Professionals & Technicians",
    "3":"Technicians & assoc. prof.","4":"Clerical support",
    "5":"Service & sales","6":"Skilled agriculture",
    "7":"Craft & trades","8":"Plant & machine operators",
    "9":"Elementary occupations"
  };
  const iscoKeys = ["1","2","3","4","5","6","7","8","9"];
  const d = isco1ds;

  const wrap = d3.create("div")
    .style("font-family","system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif")
    .style("font-size","13px").style("max-width","920px");

  /* ──── shared toggle helper ──── */
  const btnStyle = (active) =>
    `padding:6px 18px;font-size:12px;font-weight:${active?700:500};cursor:pointer;border:1px solid #cbd5e1;`
    + `background:${active?"#0f172a":"#fff"};color:${active?"#fff":"#475569"};transition:all .15s`;

  /* ──── 1. MISMATCH BAR CHART ──── */
  wrap.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","14px")
    .style("margin-bottom","10px").style("color","#0f172a")
    .text("Index of Dissimilarity by country (pp)");

  const barToggle = wrap.append("div").style("display","flex").style("gap","0").style("margin-bottom","12px");
  const barBtnEA = barToggle.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px").text("East Asia");
  const barBtnBM = barToggle.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none").text("Benchmarks");

  const barHost = wrap.append("div").style("margin-bottom","24px");

  function renderBars(countries, color) {
    barHost.html("");
    const ranked = countries
      .filter(cc => d[cc] && d[cc].mismatch != null)
      .map(cc => ({cc, m: d[cc].mismatch}))
      .sort((a,b) => a.m - b.m);
    const maxM = Math.max(...ranked.map(r => r.m), 1);

    ranked.forEach(r => {
      const row = barHost.append("div")
        .style("display","flex").style("align-items","center")
        .style("margin-bottom","3px");
      const label = (r.cc === "JP" || r.cc === "MY") ? r.cc + "*" : r.cc;
      row.append("div").style("width","34px").style("text-align","right")
        .style("font-weight","600").style("font-size","11px")
        .style("color","#0f172a").style("margin-right","8px")
        .text(label);
      const barWrap = row.append("div")
        .style("flex","1").style("height","18px")
        .style("background","#f1f5f9").style("border-radius","4px")
        .style("position","relative").style("overflow","hidden");
      barWrap.append("div")
        .style("height","100%").style("border-radius","4px")
        .style("background", color)
        .style("width", `${(r.m / (maxM * 1.1)) * 100}%`);
      row.append("div").style("width","40px").style("text-align","right")
        .style("font-size","11px").style("font-weight","600")
        .style("color","#475569").style("margin-left","6px")
        .text(r.m.toFixed(1));
    });

    const hasJP = countries.includes("JP");
    const hasMY = countries.includes("MY");
    if (hasJP || hasMY) {
      const notes = [];
      if (hasJP) notes.push("JP: Japan's JSOC merges ISCO groups 2+3; group 7 from 2023 (dropped from 2024).");
      if (hasMY) notes.push("MY: employment data from 2014 only.");
      barHost.append("div").style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8")
        .style("margin-top","6px").style("font-style","italic")
        .html("* " + notes.join(" "));
    }
  }

  renderBars(eaList, "#3b82f6");
  barBtnEA.on("click", () => {
    barBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    barBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    renderBars(eaList, "#3b82f6");
  });
  barBtnBM.on("click", () => {
    barBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    barBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    renderBars(bmList, "#94a3b8");
  });

  /* ──── 2. SKILL-LEVEL MISMATCH & POLARIZATION ──── */
  const polData = {};
  allList.forEach(cc => {
    const dem = d[cc].demand || {};
    const sup = d[cc].supply || {};
    const m23 = d[cc].merge_2_3 === true;
    const hs_d = (dem["1"]||0) + (dem["2"]||0) + (dem["3"]||0);
    const hs_e = m23
      ? (sup["1"]||0) + (sup["2"]||0)
      : (sup["1"]||0) + (sup["2"]||0) + (sup["3"]||0);
    const rm_d = ["4","5","6","7","8","9"].reduce((s,k)=>s+(dem[k]||0),0);
    const rm_e = ["4","5","6","7","8","9"].reduce((s,k)=>s+(sup[k]||0),0);
    polData[cc] = {
      hs_d: Math.round(hs_d*10)/10, hs_e: Math.round(hs_e*10)/10,
      hs_delta: Math.round((hs_d-hs_e)*10)/10,
      rm_d: Math.round(rm_d*10)/10, rm_e: Math.round(rm_e*10)/10,
      rm_delta: Math.round((rm_d-rm_e)*10)/10,
      polar: Math.round((hs_d-hs_e-(rm_d-rm_e))*10)/10
    };
  });

  wrap.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","14px")
    .style("margin","20px 0 6px").style("color","#0f172a")
    .text("Skill-level mismatch decomposition");
  wrap.append("div").style("font-size","12px").style("color","#64748b")
    .style("margin-bottom","12px")
    .html("High-skill = ISCO 1–3 (Managers, Professionals, Technicians). "
      + "Mid-/lower-skill = ISCO 4–9 (Clerical, Service, Agriculture, Craft, Operators, Elementary). "
      + "Polarization index = Δ<sub>high-skill</sub> − Δ<sub>mid-/lower-skill</sub>.");

  const polToggle = wrap.append("div").style("display","flex").style("gap","0").style("margin-bottom","12px");
  const polBtnEA = polToggle.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px").text("East Asia");
  const polBtnBM = polToggle.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none").text("Benchmarks");
  const polHost = wrap.append("div").style("margin-bottom","24px");

  function renderPolarization(countries) {
    polHost.html("");
    const sorted = [...countries].sort((a,b) => polData[b].polar - polData[a].polar);
    const maxPol = Math.max(...sorted.map(cc => Math.abs(polData[cc].polar)), 1);
    const maxDelta = Math.max(...sorted.map(cc => Math.max(Math.abs(polData[cc].hs_delta), Math.abs(polData[cc].rm_delta))), 1);

    const tbl = polHost.append("table")
      .style("border-collapse","collapse").style("width","100%").style("max-width","780px");

    const hdr = tbl.append("thead").append("tr").style("border-bottom","2px solid #e2e8f0");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","left").style("padding","6px 8px").style("font-size","11px").style("color","#64748b").text("");
    hdr.append("th").attr("colspan",3).style("text-align","center").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#2563eb").style("border-bottom","2px solid #bfdbfe").text("High-skill (ISCO 1–3)");
    hdr.append("th").attr("colspan",3).style("text-align","center").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#dc2626").style("border-bottom","2px solid #fecaca").text("Mid-/lower-skill (ISCO 4–9)");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","center").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#0f172a").style("font-weight","700").text("Polarization");

    const hdr2 = tbl.append("thead").append("tr").style("border-bottom","1px solid #e2e8f0");
    hdr2.append("th").style("padding","2px 8px");
    ["Demand","Empl.","Δ"].forEach(t => hdr2.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","2px 6px")
      .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8").text(t));
    ["Demand","Empl.","Δ"].forEach(t => hdr2.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","2px 6px")
      .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#94a3b8").text(t));
    hdr2.append("th").style("padding","2px 8px");

    const tbody = tbl.append("tbody");
    sorted.forEach((cc,i) => {
      const p = polData[cc];
      const bg = i % 2 === 0 ? "#f8fafc" : "#fff";
      const tr = tbody.append("tr").style("background", bg);

      const label = (cc === "JP" || cc === "MY") ? cc + "*" : cc;
      tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 8px").style("font-weight","600")
        .style("font-size","12px").style("white-space","nowrap").text(label);

      const numStyle = "text-align:right;padding:4px 6px;font-size:12px;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;";
      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + "color:#64748b").text(p.hs_d.toFixed(1));
      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + "color:#94a3b8").text(p.hs_e.toFixed(1));
      const hsCol = p.hs_delta > 0 ? "#16a34a" : "#dc2626";
      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + `color:${hsCol};font-weight:600`)
        .text((p.hs_delta > 0 ? "+" : "") + p.hs_delta.toFixed(1));

      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + "color:#64748b").text(p.rm_d.toFixed(1));
      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + "color:#94a3b8").text(p.rm_e.toFixed(1));
      const rmCol = p.rm_delta > 0 ? "#16a34a" : "#dc2626";
      tr.append("td").attr("style", numStyle + `color:${rmCol};font-weight:600`)
        .text((p.rm_delta > 0 ? "+" : "") + p.rm_delta.toFixed(1));

      /* polarization bar + value */
      const polTd = tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 8px").style("min-width","140px");
      const polRow = polTd.append("div").style("display","flex").style("align-items","center").style("gap","6px");
      const barW = Math.round(Math.abs(p.polar) / maxPol * 80);
      polRow.append("div").style("width", barW + "px").style("height","12px")
        .style("border-radius","3px")
        .style("background", p.polar > 60 ? "#ef4444" : p.polar > 30 ? "#f59e0b" : "#22c55e");
      polRow.append("span").style("font-size","12px").style("font-weight","700")
        .style("color","#0f172a").style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums")
        .text((p.polar > 0 ? "+" : "") + p.polar.toFixed(1));
    });

    polHost.append("div").style("margin-top","8px").style("font-size","10px")
      .style("color","#94a3b8").style("line-height","1.6")
      .html("Polarization index = Δ<sub>high-skill</sub> − Δ<sub>mid-/lower-skill</sub>. "
        + "Higher values indicate that online demand is more skewed toward high-skill occupations relative to actual employment. "
        + '<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:2px;background:#22c55e;vertical-align:middle"></span> &lt; 30 pp &ensp;'
        + '<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:2px;background:#f59e0b;vertical-align:middle"></span> 30–60 pp &ensp;'
        + '<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:2px;background:#ef4444;vertical-align:middle"></span> &gt; 60 pp');
  }

  renderPolarization(eaList);
  polBtnEA.on("click", () => {
    polBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    polBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    renderPolarization(eaList);
  });
  polBtnBM.on("click", () => {
    polBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    polBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    renderPolarization(bmList);
  });

  /* ──── 3. COUNTRY DETAIL TABLE ──── */
  wrap.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","14px")
    .style("margin","8px 0 10px").style("color","#0f172a")
    .text("Detailed ISCO breakdown by country");

  const ctrl = wrap.append("div")
    .style("display","flex").style("gap","10px")
    .style("align-items","center").style("margin-bottom","12px")
    .style("flex-wrap","wrap");

  const detBtnBar = ctrl.append("div").style("display","flex").style("gap","0");
  const detBtnEA = detBtnBar.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px").text("East Asia");
  const detBtnBM = detBtnBar.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none").text("Benchmarks");

  ctrl.append("label").style("font-weight","600").style("font-size","12px").text("Country:");
  const selCC = ctrl.append("select")
    .style("padding","5px 10px").style("border","1px solid #cbd5e1")
    .style("border-radius","6px").style("font-size","12px");

  function populateSelect(countries) {
    selCC.selectAll("option").remove();
    countries.forEach(cc => {
      const yr = d[cc] ? (d[cc].supply_year || "n/a") : "n/a";
      const star = (cc === "JP" || cc === "MY") ? " *" : "";
      selCC.append("option").attr("value", cc)
        .text(`${countryFull[cc]} (${cc})${star} — empl. ${yr}`);
    });
  }
  populateSelect(eaList);

  detBtnEA.on("click", () => {
    detBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    detBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    populateSelect(eaList);
    renderDetail(eaList[0]);
  });
  detBtnBM.on("click", () => {
    detBtnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    detBtnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    populateSelect(bmList);
    renderDetail(bmList[0]);
  });

  const detailHost = wrap.append("div");

  function renderDetail(cc) {
    detailHost.html("");
    const cd = d[cc];
    if (!cd) return;
    const dem = cd.demand || {};
    const sup = cd.supply || {};
    const yr = cd.supply_year || "n/a";
    const mm = cd.mismatch;
    const merging = cd.merge_2_3 === true;

    /* mismatch badge */
    const badge = detailHost.append("div")
      .style("display","inline-block").style("padding","6px 14px")
      .style("border-radius","8px").style("margin-bottom","10px")
      .style("background", mm > 50 ? "#fef2f2" : mm > 30 ? "#fffbeb" : "#f0fdf4")
      .style("border", `1px solid ${mm > 50 ? "#fca5a5" : mm > 30 ? "#fde68a" : "#86efac"}`);
    badge.append("span").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","18px")
      .style("color","#0f172a").text(mm != null ? mm.toFixed(1) : "n/a");
    badge.append("span").style("font-size","11px").style("color","#64748b")
      .style("margin-left","6px").text("pp mismatch");

    /* table */
    const tbl = detailHost.append("table")
      .style("border-collapse","collapse").style("width","100%")
      .style("max-width","640px");

    const hdr = tbl.append("thead").append("tr")
      .style("border-bottom","2px solid #e2e8f0");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","left").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#64748b").text("ISCO-08 Major Group");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#3b82f6").text("Demand (%)");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#64748b").text(`Empl. (%) ${yr}`);
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","right").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#475569").text("Gap (pp)");
    hdr.append("th").style("text-align","left").style("padding","6px 8px")
      .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#94a3b8").style("width","120px").text("");

    const tbody = tbl.append("tbody");
    const barScale = 30;

    const rowKeys = merging
      ? ["1","2+3","4","5","6","7","8","9"]
      : iscoKeys;

    rowKeys.forEach((k,i) => {
      let di, ei, label;
      if (k === "2+3") {
        di = (dem["2"] || 0) + (dem["3"] || 0);
        ei = sup["2"] || 0;
        label = "2+3  " + iscoNames["2+3"];
      } else {
        di = dem[k] || 0;
        ei = sup[k] || 0;
        label = k + "  " + (iscoNames[k] || k);
      }
      const hasSup = ei > 0;
      const gap = hasSup ? di - ei : null;
      const gapColor = gap == null ? "#94a3b8" : gap > 2 ? "#16a34a" : gap < -2 ? "#dc2626" : "#64748b";
      const bg = i % 2 === 0 ? "#f8fafc" : "#fff";

      const tr = tbody.append("tr").style("background",bg);
      tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 8px").style("font-size","12px")
        .style("white-space","nowrap").text(label);
      tr.append("td").style("text-align","right").style("padding","4px 8px")
        .style("font-size","12px").style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums")
        .style("color","#0f172a").text(di.toFixed(1));
      tr.append("td").style("text-align","right").style("padding","4px 8px")
        .style("font-size","12px").style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums")
        .style("color","#64748b").text(hasSup ? ei.toFixed(1) : "n/a");
      tr.append("td").style("text-align","right").style("padding","4px 8px")
        .style("font-size","12px").style("font-weight","600")
        .style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums")
        .style("color", gapColor)
        .text(gap != null ? (gap > 0 ? "+" : "") + gap.toFixed(1) : "—");

      /* mini diverging bar */
      const barTd = tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 8px");
      if (gap != null) {
        const barDiv = barTd.append("div")
          .style("display","flex").style("align-items","center")
          .style("height","12px");
        const mid = 60;
        const barW = Math.min(Math.abs(gap) / barScale * mid, mid);
        if (gap < 0) {
          barDiv.append("div").style("flex","1").style("display","flex")
            .style("justify-content","flex-end")
            .append("div").style("height","8px").style("border-radius","2px")
            .style("background","#fca5a5").style("width", barW + "px");
          barDiv.append("div").style("width","1px").style("height","12px")
            .style("background","#cbd5e1");
          barDiv.append("div").style("flex","1");
        } else {
          barDiv.append("div").style("flex","1");
          barDiv.append("div").style("width","1px").style("height","12px")
            .style("background","#cbd5e1");
          barDiv.append("div").style("flex","1").style("display","flex")
            .style("justify-content","flex-start")
            .append("div").style("height","8px").style("border-radius","2px")
            .style("background","#86efac").style("width", barW + "px");
        }
      }
    });

    /* country-specific caveats */
    if (cc === "JP") {
      detailHost.append("div").style("margin-top","6px").style("font-size","10px")
        .style("color","#94a3b8").style("font-style","italic")
        .html("Note: Japan's Standard Occupational Classification (JSOC) merges ISCO groups 2 and 3 into one category. "
          + "Groups 2+3 are shown combined for comparability. Group 7 last reported in 2023 (merged into 8 from 2024). "
          + "Mismatch index computed on comparable groups.");
    }
    if (cc === "MY") {
      detailHost.append("div").style("margin-top","6px").style("font-size","10px")
        .style("color","#94a3b8").style("font-style","italic")
        .text("Note: Malaysia's latest available ILOSTAT data is from 2014. The mismatch index may be affected by structural change since then.");
    }
  }

  renderDetail("JP");
  selCC.on("change", function() { renderDetail(this.value); });

  /* ──── 3. FOOTNOTE ──── */
  const fn = wrap.append("div").style("margin-top","14px").style("font-size","10px")
    .style("color","#94a3b8").style("line-height","1.6");
  fn.html(`<b>Demand</b> = share of online job postings (this study, 03.2025–03.2026). `
    + `<b>Employment</b> = share of total employment from national labour force surveys reported to `
    + `<a class="paper-link" href="https://ilostat.ilo.org/topics/employment/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ILOSTAT<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span></a> `
    + `(indicator <code>EMP_TEMP_SEX_OCU_NB</code>, latest available year per country). `
    + `<b>Gap</b> = demand − employment (pp). `
    + `Green bars indicate demand exceeds employment; red bars indicate employment exceeds demand. `
    + `Taiwan excluded (no ISCO-08 data in ILOSTAT). `
    + `Malaysia: employment data from 2014 only. `
    + `Japan: JSOC merges ISCO 2+3; group 7 from 2023 (dropped from 2024). Mismatch computed on comparable groups.`);

  return wrap.node();
}
```

#### Top occupation groups by sector

The table below shows the five most frequent ISCO-08 occupation groups
(4-digit level) within each ISIC/NACE section, per country. Use the
selectors to browse sectors and compare across countries.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

isco4data = FileAttachment("nace_isco4_top5.json").json()

{
  const eaList = ["JP","KR","TW","TH","MY","SG","ID","VN","PH"];
  const bmList = ["IN","MX","PL"];
  const countryFull = {JP:"Japan",KR:"S. Korea",TW:"Taiwan",TH:"Thailand",MY:"Malaysia",SG:"Singapore",ID:"Indonesia",VN:"Vietnam",PH:"Philippines",IN:"India",MX:"Mexico",PL:"Poland"};
  const d = isco4data;

  const sectionKeys = Object.keys(d).sort();
  const sectionOpts = sectionKeys.map(k => ({key:k, label:`${k}  ${d[k].name}`}));

  const wrap = d3.create("div")
    .style("font-family","system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif")
    .style("font-size","12px");

  /* ── controls ── */
  const ctrl = wrap.append("div")
    .style("display","flex").style("gap","16px")
    .style("align-items","center").style("margin-bottom","12px")
    .style("flex-wrap","wrap");

  /* toggle East Asia / Benchmarks */
  const btnBar = ctrl.append("div").style("display","flex").style("gap","0");
  const btnStyle = (active) =>
    `padding:5px 14px;font-size:11px;font-weight:${active?700:500};cursor:pointer;border:1px solid #cbd5e1;`
    + `background:${active?"#0f172a":"#fff"};color:${active?"#fff":"#475569"};transition:all .15s`;
  const btnEA = btnBar.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px").text("East Asia");
  const btnBM = btnBar.append("button").attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none").text("Benchmarks");

  ctrl.append("label").style("font-weight","600").style("font-size","12px").text("Sector:");
  const selSect = ctrl.append("select")
    .style("padding","4px 8px").style("border","1px solid #cbd5e1")
    .style("border-radius","6px").style("font-size","12px");
  sectionOpts.forEach(o => {
    selSect.append("option").attr("value", o.key).text(o.label);
  });
  selSect.property("value", "N");

  const tableHost = wrap.append("div");

  let activeGroup = "ea";

  function shortOcc(name, n) {
    return name.length > n ? name.slice(0, n - 1) + "\u2026" : name;
  }

  function render(letter) {
    tableHost.html("");
    const sect = d[letter];
    if (!sect) return;

    const countries = activeGroup === "ea" ? eaList : bmList;
    const barMax = 60;

    const grid = tableHost.append("div")
      .style("display","grid")
      .style("grid-template-columns","repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr))")
      .style("gap","10px");

    countries.forEach(cc => {
      const cd = sect.countries[cc];
      if (!cd || cd.total === 0) return;

      const card = grid.append("div")
        .style("border","1px solid #e2e8f0").style("border-radius","8px")
        .style("padding","8px 12px").style("background","#fff");

      card.append("div")
        .style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","12px").style("color","#0f172a")
        .style("margin-bottom","4px")
        .html(`${countryFull[cc]} <span style="font-weight:400;color:#94a3b8;font-size:11px">${cc} \u00b7 ${cd.total.toLocaleString()} postings</span>`);

      const tbl = card.append("table")
        .style("width","100%").style("border-collapse","collapse");

      cd.top.forEach((item, idx) => {
        const tr = tbl.append("tr");
        tr.append("td")
          .style("padding","2px 4px").style("font-size","10px")
          .style("color","#94a3b8").style("width","36px")
          .style("text-align","right").style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums")
          .text(item.code);
        const nameTd = tr.append("td")
          .style("padding","2px 6px").style("font-size","11px");
        nameTd.append("div").style("margin-bottom","1px").text(shortOcc(item.name, 40));
        nameTd.append("div").style("display","flex").style("align-items","center").style("gap","4px");
        nameTd.select("div:last-child").append("div")
          .style("height","6px").style("border-radius","3px")
          .style("background","#3b82f6")
          .style("width", `${Math.max(item.pct / barMax * 100, 2)}%`)
          .style("min-width","3px");
        nameTd.select("div:last-child").append("span")
          .style("font-size","10px").style("color","#64748b")
          .style("white-space","nowrap")
          .text(`${item.pct}%`);
      });
    });
  }

  render("N");
  selSect.on("change", function() { render(this.value); });
  btnEA.on("click", () => {
    activeGroup = "ea";
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    render(selSect.property("value"));
  });
  btnBM.on("click", () => {
    activeGroup = "bm";
    btnEA.attr("style", btnStyle(false)+";border-radius:6px 0 0 6px");
    btnBM.attr("style", btnStyle(true)+";border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;border-left:none");
    render(selSect.property("value"));
  });

  return wrap.node();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  /* ───────── COUNTRY PROFILES ───────── */
  const profiles = {
    JP: {full:"Japan", n:"296,749", over:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:3.0,r:2.5},{s:"F",n:"Construction",p:4.2,r:2.3},{s:"E",n:"Water & waste",p:0.7,r:2.2}], under:[{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:0.5,r:0.2},{s:"N",n:"Professional & scientific",p:8.4,r:0.4}]},
    KR: {full:"S. Korea", n:"132,148", over:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:8.2,r:6.8},{s:"R",n:"Health & social work",p:13.5,r:3.2},{s:"C",n:"Manufacturing",p:14.5,r:2.0}], under:[{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:2.7,r:0.4},{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:1.0,r:0.4}]},
    TW: {full:"Taiwan", n:"55,217", over:[{s:"F",n:"Construction",p:3.6,r:2.0},{s:"C",n:"Manufacturing",p:12.8,r:1.8},{s:"A",n:"Agriculture",p:1.4,r:1.7}], under:[{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:2.5,r:0.4},{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:1.4,r:0.5}]},
    TH: {full:"Thailand", n:"57,892", over:[{s:"I",n:"Accommodation & food",p:24.4,r:2.8},{s:"H",n:"Transport & storage",p:9.1,r:1.5},{s:"S",n:"Arts, sports & recreation",p:3.6,r:1.3}], under:[{s:"R",n:"Health & social work",p:1.0,r:0.2},{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:2.7,r:0.4}]},
    MY: {full:"Malaysia", n:"171,586", over:[{s:"I",n:"Accommodation & food",p:14.2,r:1.6},{s:"J",n:"Publishing & broadcasting",p:1.9,r:1.3},{s:"S",n:"Arts, sports & recreation",p:3.6,r:1.3}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.4,r:0.3},{s:"E",n:"Water & waste",p:0.2,r:0.6}]},
    SG: {full:"Singapore", n:"138,202", over:[{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:10.3,r:1.5},{s:"N",n:"Professional & scientific",p:32.8,r:1.5},{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:3.7,r:1.4}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.1,r:0.1},{s:"E",n:"Water & waste",p:0.1,r:0.3}]},
    ID: {full:"Indonesia", n:"113,460", over:[{s:"I",n:"Accommodation & food",p:13.6,r:1.6},{s:"N",n:"Professional & scientific",p:28.2,r:1.3},{s:"S",n:"Arts, sports & recreation",p:3.4,r:1.3}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.1,r:0.1},{s:"R",n:"Health & social work",p:1.2,r:0.3}]},
    VN: {full:"Vietnam", n:"145,549", over:[{s:"J",n:"Publishing & broadcasting",p:2.4,r:1.7},{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:4.5,r:1.7},{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:10.2,r:1.5}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.2,r:0.2},{s:"R",n:"Health & social work",p:1.2,r:0.3}]},
    PH: {full:"Philippines", n:"187,818", over:[{s:"M",n:"Real estate",p:1.2,r:1.5},{s:"O",n:"Admin & support",p:16.2,r:1.4},{s:"N",n:"Professional & scientific",p:27.8,r:1.3}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.4,r:0.3},{s:"F",n:"Construction",p:1.0,r:0.6}]}
  };
  const benchProfiles = {
    IN: {full:"India", n:"224,187", over:[{s:"J",n:"Publishing & broadcasting",p:3.2,r:2.2},{s:"Q",n:"Education",p:7.6,r:2.1},{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:11.4,r:1.7}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.2,r:0.2},{s:"E",n:"Water & waste",p:0.1,r:0.3}]},
    MX: {full:"Mexico", n:"199,404", over:[{s:"L",n:"Financial & insurance",p:6.1,r:2.3},{s:"H",n:"Transport & storage",p:10.1,r:1.7},{s:"T",n:"Other services",p:3.6,r:1.5}], under:[{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.3,r:0.2},{s:"J",n:"Publishing & broadcasting",p:0.4,r:0.3}]},
    PL: {full:"Poland", n:"135,027", over:[{s:"K",n:"Telecom & IT",p:12.0,r:1.8},{s:"G",n:"Wholesale & retail",p:18.5,r:1.5},{s:"T",n:"Other services",p:3.4,r:1.4}], under:[{s:"I",n:"Accommodation & food",p:1.5,r:0.2},{s:"U",n:"Households",p:0.4,r:0.3}]}
  };

  const overColor = "#dcfce7";
  const underColor = "#fee2e2";

  function makeCard(card, p) {
    card.append("div")
      .style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","13px")
      .style("margin-bottom","2px").style("color","#0f172a")
      .html(`${p.full} <span style="font-weight:400;color:#64748b">(${p.n} postings)</span>`);
    const tbl = card.append("table")
      .style("width","100%").style("border-collapse","collapse").style("margin-top","6px");
    const renderRow = (item, bg) => {
      const tr = tbl.append("tr");
      tr.append("td").style("padding","1px 4px").style("border-radius","4px")
        .style("background",bg).style("font-weight","600")
        .style("text-align","center").style("width","36px").text(`${item.r}\u00d7`);
      tr.append("td").style("padding","1px 6px").text(`${item.s} ${item.n}`);
      tr.append("td").style("padding","1px 4px").style("text-align","right")
        .style("color","#64748b").text(`${item.p}%`);
    };
    p.over.forEach(item => renderRow(item, overColor));
    p.under.forEach(item => renderRow(item, underColor));
  }

  const wrap = d3.create("div").style("font-family","system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif").style("font-size","12px");

  wrap.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","14px")
    .style("margin","0.6rem 0 8px").style("color","#0f172a").text("East Asia");
  const eaGrid = wrap.append("div")
    .style("display","grid").style("grid-template-columns","repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr))")
    .style("gap","12px");
  for (const [cc, p] of Object.entries(profiles)) {
    const card = eaGrid.append("div")
      .style("border","1px solid #e2e8f0").style("border-radius","10px")
      .style("padding","10px 14px").style("background","#fff");
    makeCard(card, p);
  }

  wrap.append("div").style("font-weight","700").style("font-size","14px")
    .style("margin","1.2rem 0 8px").style("color","#64748b").text("Benchmark countries");
  const bmGrid = wrap.append("div")
    .style("display","grid").style("grid-template-columns","repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr))")
    .style("gap","12px");
  for (const [cc, p] of Object.entries(benchProfiles)) {
    const card = bmGrid.append("div")
      .style("border","1px solid #e2e8f0").style("border-radius","10px")
      .style("padding","10px 14px").style("background","#fff")
      .style("border-color","#cbd5e1");
    makeCard(card, p);
  }

  return wrap.node();
}
```

The cards above show each country's **most distinctive sectors**
relative to the 12-country average. Green values indicate
overrepresentation ($> 1\times$ average), red values indicate
underrepresentation ($< 1\times$ average).

:::

### Skill composition

::: {.section-card}
#### Cross-country comparison

The charts below compare the composition of mapped ESCO concepts across
three selected countries. Shares are normalised within each country
separately for **skills** and **knowledge**, so each column sums to 100%
within the respective panel. The knowledge panel shows the most frequent
3-digit knowledge categories. Language and other ESCO concept types are
excluded from this specific view.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

categoryData = FileAttachment("esco_category_shares.json").json()

{
  const data = await categoryData;
  const countries = data.country_order;
  const eastAsiaButtons = [
    { key: "Japan", label: "Japan", enabled: true },
    { key: "South Korea", label: "South Korea", enabled: true },
    { key: "Singapore", label: "Singapore", enabled: true },
    { key: "Taiwan", label: "Taiwan", enabled: true },
    { key: "Malaysia", label: "Malaysia", enabled: true },
    { key: "Thailand", label: "Thailand", enabled: true },
    { key: "Indonesia", label: "Indonesia", enabled: true },
    { key: "Vietnam", label: "Vietnam", enabled: true },
    { key: "Philippines", label: "Philippines", enabled: true },
  ];
  const benchmarkButtons = [
    { key: "India", label: "India", enabled: true },
    { key: "Mexico", label: "Mexico", enabled: true },
    { key: "Poland", label: "Poland", enabled: true },
  ];
  const countryButtons = [...eastAsiaButtons, ...benchmarkButtons];
  const eastAsiaCountries = eastAsiaButtons.map(d => d.key);
  const benchmarkCountries = benchmarkButtons.map(d => d.key);
  const regionPalette = {
    "East Asia": "#2563eb",
    "Benchmark": "#7c3aed"
  };
  let layoutMode = "separate";
  let selectedCountries = ["Japan", "Singapore", "Indonesia", "India", "Poland"];
  const skillCodes = ["S1", "S2", "S3", "S4", "S5", "S6", "S7", "S8"];

  const wrap = d3.create("div");

  const controls = wrap.append("div").attr("class", "compare-controls");
  const box = controls.append("div").attr("class", "compare-control");
  box.append("label").text("Select up to 5 countries");
  const buttonGroup = box.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-wrap");

  const eaGroup = buttonGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group");
  eaGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-label").text("East Asia");
  const eaPills = eaGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-pills");

  const bmGroup = buttonGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group");
  bmGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-label").text("Benchmark");
  const bmPills = bmGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-pills");

  const viewBox = controls.append("div").attr("class", "compare-control");
  viewBox.append("label").text("Show");
  const viewToggle = viewBox.append("div").attr("class", "view-toggle-group");

  wrap.append("div")
    .attr("class", "compare-note")
    .text("Each cell shows the share of the selected country in the most aggregated ESCO skill and knowledge groups. You can compare up to five countries at once and switch between an East Asia vs benchmark view and an all-countries view.");

  const chartHost = wrap.append("div").attr("class", "compare-charts");

  const shortLabel = (text, n = 44) => {
    if (text === "information and communication technologies (icts)") return "ICT";
    return text.length > n ? `${text.slice(0, n - 1)}…` : text;
  };

  const regionForCountry = country => (
    benchmarkCountries.includes(country) ? "Benchmark" : "East Asia"
  );

  const orderSelectedCountries = selected => {
    if (layoutMode === "combined") return [...selected];
    return [
      ...selected.filter(country => regionForCountry(country) === "East Asia"),
      ...selected.filter(country => regionForCountry(country) === "Benchmark"),
    ];
  };

  function drawHeatmap({rows, selected, paletteInterpolator}) {
    const eastAsiaSelected = selected.filter(country => regionForCountry(country) === "East Asia");
    const benchmarkSelected = selected.filter(country => regionForCountry(country) === "Benchmark");
    const showSeparatedGroups = layoutMode === "separate" && eastAsiaSelected.length && benchmarkSelected.length;
    const groupGap = showSeparatedGroups ? 28 : 0;
    const margin = {top: showSeparatedGroups ? 52 : 34, right: 14, bottom: 10, left: 270};
    const cellW = 92;
    const cellH = 30;
    const width = margin.left + selected.length * cellW + groupGap + margin.right;
    const height = margin.top + rows.length * cellH + margin.bottom;
    const maxShare = d3.max(rows.flatMap(r => selected.map(c => r.values[c] || 0))) || 0.001;
    const color = d3.scaleSequential([0, maxShare], paletteInterpolator);
    const xForColumn = i => {
      const isBenchmarkBlock = showSeparatedGroups && i >= eastAsiaSelected.length;
      return margin.left + i * cellW + (isBenchmarkBlock ? groupGap : 0);
    };

    const svg = d3.create("svg")
      .attr("viewBox", [0, 0, width, height])
      .attr("width", width)
      .attr("height", height)
      .style("max-width", "100%")
      .style("height", "auto");

    if (showSeparatedGroups) {
      const groups = [
        { label: "East Asia", count: eastAsiaSelected.length, offset: 0 },
        { label: "Benchmark", count: benchmarkSelected.length, offset: eastAsiaSelected.length },
      ];
      groups.forEach(group => {
        const startX = xForColumn(group.offset);
        const endX = xForColumn(group.offset + group.count - 1) + cellW - 4;
        svg.append("text")
          .attr("x", (startX + endX) / 2)
          .attr("y", 31)
          .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
          .attr("font-size", 11)
          .attr("font-weight", 800)
          .attr("letter-spacing", "0.06em")
          .attr("fill", regionPalette[group.label])
          .text(group.label.toUpperCase());
      });
    }

    selected.forEach((country, i) => {
      const xCenter = xForColumn(i) + cellW / 2;
      svg.append("text")
        .attr("x", xCenter)
        .attr("y", margin.top - 10)
        .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
        .attr("font-size", 12)
        .attr("font-weight", 700)
        .attr("fill", "#334155")
        .text(country);
    });

    rows.forEach((row, r) => {
      const y = margin.top + r * cellH;

      const rowLabel = svg.append("text")
        .attr("x", margin.left - 10)
        .attr("y", y + cellH / 2 + 4)
        .attr("text-anchor", "end")
        .attr("font-size", 12);

      rowLabel.append("tspan")
        .attr("fill", "#94a3b8")
        .attr("font-weight", 600)
        .text(`${row.code} `);

      rowLabel.append("tspan")
        .attr("fill", "#334155")
        .attr("font-weight", 700)
        .text(row.label);

      selected.forEach((country, c) => {
        const value = row.values[country] || 0;
        const x = xForColumn(c);
        svg.append("rect")
          .attr("x", x)
          .attr("y", y)
          .attr("width", cellW - 4)
          .attr("height", cellH - 4)
          .attr("rx", 6)
          .attr("fill", color(value))
          .attr("stroke", "#ffffff");
        svg.append("text")
          .attr("x", x + (cellW - 4) / 2)
          .attr("y", y + cellH / 2 + 4)
          .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
          .attr("font-size", 11)
          .attr("font-weight", 700)
          .attr("fill", value > maxShare * 0.45 ? "#ffffff" : "#0f172a")
          .text(d3.format(".1%")(value));
      });
    });

    return svg.node();
  }

  function render() {
    const selected = orderSelectedCountries(selectedCountries);
    chartHost.selectAll("*").remove();

    const skillRows = skillCodes.map(code => ({
      code,
      label: shortLabel(data.skill_meta[code] || code, 38),
      values: Object.fromEntries(selected.map(country => [country, data.countries[country]?.skill_shares?.[code] || 0]))
    }));

    const knowledgeRows = [...data.top_knowledge_codes]
      .map(code => ({
        code,
        label: shortLabel(data.knowledge_meta[code] || code, 42),
        values: Object.fromEntries(selected.map(country => [country, data.countries[country]?.knowledge_shares?.[code] || 0]))
      }))
      .sort((a, b) =>
        d3.mean(selected.map(country => b.values[country] || 0)) -
        d3.mean(selected.map(country => a.values[country] || 0))
      );

    const skillPanel = chartHost.append("div");
    skillPanel.append("div")
      .attr("class", "compare-panel-title")
      .text("Skills");
    skillPanel.append(() => drawHeatmap({
      rows: skillRows,
      selected,
      paletteInterpolator: t => d3.interpolateRgb("#eff6ff", "#2563eb")(t)
    }));

    const knowledgePanel = chartHost.append("div");
    knowledgePanel.append("div")
      .attr("class", "compare-panel-title")
      .text("Knowledge");
    knowledgePanel.append(() => drawHeatmap({
      rows: knowledgeRows,
      selected,
      paletteInterpolator: t => d3.interpolateRgb("#fff7ed", "#ea580c")(t)
    }));
  }

  const pillClass = d => `country-pill${d.enabled ? "" : " disabled"}${selectedCountries.includes(d.key) ? " active" : ""}`;

  const handleClick = function(event, d) {
    if (!d.enabled) return;
    const isSelected = selectedCountries.includes(d.key);
    if (isSelected) {
      if (selectedCountries.length === 1) return;
      selectedCountries = selectedCountries.filter(v => v !== d.key);
    } else {
      if (selectedCountries.length >= 5) return;
      selectedCountries = [...selectedCountries, d.key];
    }
    allButtons.attr("class", pillClass);
    render();
  };

  const eaButtons = eaPills.selectAll("button")
    .data(eastAsiaButtons)
    .join("button")
      .attr("type", "button")
      .attr("class", pillClass)
      .property("disabled", d => !d.enabled)
      .text(d => d.label)
      .on("click", handleClick);

  const bmButtons = bmPills.selectAll("button")
    .data(benchmarkButtons)
    .join("button")
      .attr("type", "button")
      .attr("class", pillClass)
      .property("disabled", d => !d.enabled)
      .text(d => d.label)
      .on("click", handleClick);

  const allButtons = d3.selectAll([...eaButtons.nodes(), ...bmButtons.nodes()]);

  const toggleLabel = () => (
    layoutMode === "separate" ? "Show combined" : "Show East Asia / Benchmark"
  );
  const toggleButtons = viewToggle.selectAll("button")
    .data([0])
    .join("button")
      .attr("type", "button")
      .attr("class", "view-toggle-btn active")
      .text(() => toggleLabel())
      .on("click", function() {
        layoutMode = layoutMode === "separate" ? "combined" : "separate";
        toggleButtons.text(() => toggleLabel());
        render();
      });

  render();

  return wrap.node();
}
```
:::

### Digital skills

::: {.section-card}
#### Digital skills prevalence

Using the digital skill classification from
<a class="paper-link" href="https://ideas.repec.org/p/wbk/hdgens/203091.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Angel-Urdinola et al. (2025)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span><span class="paper-tooltip" role="tooltip">Angel-Urdinola, D., Chinen, M. H., &amp; Rodon Casarramona, G. (2025). <em>Digital Skills Development: Competence Frameworks, Assessment Tools, and Pedagogical Approaches.</em> <em>Education Notes</em>, (203091).</span></a>, we
identify ESCO skills associated with **basic**, **intermediate**, and
**advanced** digital capabilities. In broad terms, **basic** digital
skills cover routine use of standard software and digital tools,
**intermediate** skills include working with specialised business or ICT
applications, and **advanced** skills refer to tasks such as software
development, data science, AI, or advanced systems engineering. The
chart below shows the composition of identified digital skill mentions
within each country, so the three segments sum to 100% for every bar.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

digitalData = FileAttachment("digital_skill_offer_shares.json").json()

{
  const data = await digitalData;
  const levelOrder = ["Basic", "Intermediate", "Advanced"];
  const eastAsiaCountries = new Set([
    "Japan", "South Korea", "Singapore", "Taiwan",
    "Malaysia", "Thailand", "Indonesia", "Vietnam", "Philippines"
  ]);
  const regionPalette = {
    "East Asia": "#2563eb",
    "Benchmark": "#7c3aed"
  };
  let layoutMode = "separate";
  const palette = {
    Basic: "#93c5fd",
    Intermediate: "#3b82f6",
    Advanced: "#1d4ed8"
  };
  const regionForCountry = country => (
    eastAsiaCountries.has(country) ? "East Asia" : "Benchmark"
  );

  const baseRows = [...data.rows]
    .map(d => ({
      country: d.country,
      Basic: d.shares.Basic,
      Intermediate: d.shares.Intermediate,
      Advanced: d.shares.Advanced
    }));

  const wrap = d3.create("div");
  const controls = wrap.append("div").attr("class", "compare-controls");
  const viewControl = controls.append("div").attr("class", "compare-control");
  viewControl.append("label").text("Show");
  const viewToggle = viewControl.append("div").attr("class", "view-toggle-group");
  wrap.append("div")
    .attr("class", "compare-note")
    .text("Switch between an East Asia vs benchmark view and a single all-countries ranking.");
  const chartHost = wrap.append("div");

  const orderedRows = () => {
    const byAdvanced = (a, b) => b.Advanced - a.Advanced;
    if (layoutMode === "combined") {
      return {
        rows: [...baseRows].sort(byAdvanced),
        groups: [],
      };
    }
    const eaRows = baseRows
      .filter(row => regionForCountry(row.country) === "East Asia")
      .sort(byAdvanced);
    const benchmarkRows = baseRows
      .filter(row => regionForCountry(row.country) === "Benchmark")
      .sort(byAdvanced);
    return {
      rows: [...eaRows, ...benchmarkRows],
      groups: [
        { label: "East Asia", count: eaRows.length, startIndex: 0 },
        { label: "Benchmark", count: benchmarkRows.length, startIndex: eaRows.length },
      ].filter(group => group.count > 0),
    };
  };

  function render() {
    const { rows, groups } = orderedRows();
    const margin = {top: 34, right: 24, bottom: 28, left: 150};
    const rowH = 30;
    const width = 860;
    const groupGap = layoutMode === "separate" && groups.length > 1 ? 22 : 0;
    const benchmarkStartIndex = layoutMode === "separate" && groups.length > 1 ? groups[1].startIndex : null;
    const yForRow = i => margin.top + i * rowH + (benchmarkStartIndex != null && i >= benchmarkStartIndex ? groupGap : 0);
    const height = margin.top + rows.length * rowH + groupGap + margin.bottom;

    chartHost.selectAll("*").remove();

    const svg = d3.create("svg")
      .attr("viewBox", [0, 0, width, height])
      .attr("width", width)
      .attr("height", height)
      .style("max-width", "100%")
      .style("height", "auto");

    const x = d3.scaleLinear()
      .domain([0, 1])
      .range([margin.left, width - margin.right]);

    const legend = svg.append("g")
      .attr("transform", `translate(${margin.left}, 14)`);

    levelOrder.forEach((level, i) => {
      const g = legend.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${i * 160}, 0)`);
      g.append("rect")
        .attr("width", 14)
        .attr("height", 14)
        .attr("rx", 3)
        .attr("fill", palette[level]);
      g.append("text")
        .attr("x", 20)
        .attr("y", 11)
        .attr("font-size", 12)
        .attr("font-weight", 700)
        .attr("fill", "#334155")
        .text(level);
    });

    if (layoutMode === "separate") {
      groups.forEach(group => {
        const y = yForRow(group.startIndex) - 8;
        svg.append("text")
          .attr("x", margin.left - 12)
          .attr("y", y)
          .attr("text-anchor", "end")
          .attr("font-size", 11)
          .attr("font-weight", 800)
          .attr("letter-spacing", "0.06em")
          .attr("fill", regionPalette[group.label])
          .text(group.label.toUpperCase());
      });
    }

    rows.forEach((row, i) => {
      const y0 = yForRow(i);

      svg.append("text")
        .attr("x", margin.left - 12)
        .attr("y", y0 + 17)
        .attr("text-anchor", "end")
        .attr("font-size", 12)
        .attr("font-weight", 700)
        .attr("fill", "#334155")
        .text(row.country);

      let x0 = margin.left;
      levelOrder.forEach((level, j) => {
        const value = row[level];
        const w = x(value) - x(0);
        svg.append("rect")
          .attr("x", x0)
          .attr("y", y0 + 5)
          .attr("width", w)
          .attr("height", 20)
          .attr("rx", 6)
          .attr("fill", palette[level]);

        if (w > 54) {
          svg.append("text")
            .attr("x", x0 + w / 2)
            .attr("y", y0 + 19)
            .attr("text-anchor", "middle")
            .attr("font-size", 11)
            .attr("font-weight", 700)
            .attr("fill", j === 0 ? "#1e3a8a" : "#ffffff")
            .text(d3.format(".1%")(value));
        }
        x0 += w;
      });
    });

    chartHost.append(() => svg.node());
  }

  const toggleLabel = () => (
    layoutMode === "separate" ? "Show combined" : "Show East Asia / Benchmark"
  );
  const toggleButtons = viewToggle.selectAll("button")
    .data([0])
    .join("button")
      .attr("type", "button")
      .attr("class", "view-toggle-btn active")
      .text(() => toggleLabel())
      .on("click", function() {
        layoutMode = layoutMode === "separate" ? "combined" : "separate";
        toggleButtons.text(() => toggleLabel());
        render();
      });

  render();
  return wrap.node();
}
```
:::

::: {.section-card}
#### Digital skill composition in East Asia

Across all twelve countries, advanced ICT skills dominate the digital
skill mix, typically accounting for 50-72% of all classified digital
skill mentions. This pattern is unlikely to reflect the full spectrum
of digital capability used at work. Rather, it suggests that basic
digital skills such as office applications, email, and web use are
increasingly taken for granted by employers and therefore rarely listed
explicitly, whereas more specialised requirements are more likely to be
spelled out in online job postings.

The country profiles below show that East Asia is not characterised by
a single model of digital demand. Instead, the composition of advanced
digital skills points to several distinct trajectories. **Japan** and
**Vietnam** are more strongly oriented toward software development and
digital production workflows. **South Korea** and **Taiwan** display a
more engineering- and hardware-intensive pattern, consistent with their
electronics and semiconductor ecosystems. **Singapore** stands out as a
cloud- and platform-oriented digital hub, while **Malaysia** and **the
Philippines** show a more mixed profile in which digital demand is more
closely tied to business systems, reporting, administration, and
service delivery. **Indonesia** occupies an intermediate position, with
stronger links to business ICT systems and mobile ecosystems, while
**Thailand** shows a more diffuse profile without one dominant advanced
specialisation.

These differences matter because they suggest that similar headline
shares of advanced digital skills may mask very different forms of
structural transformation. In some countries, digitalisation is closely
linked to software engineering and data-intensive production. In
others, it is more closely associated with digitally enabled
manufacturing, enterprise systems, or service-sector process
management. The lower section of this report shows that it is the
*content* of advanced demand, rather than its aggregate share alone,
that most clearly differentiates country profiles.

Among the benchmark countries, **Poland** is closest to Singapore in
its overall advanced orientation, while **India** combines broad IT
capabilities with a wider services and digital content ecosystem.
**Mexico** sits closer to the business-process digitalisation pattern,
with a stronger mix of advanced and intermediate business tools.

Basic digital skills remain a minority component everywhere (10-21%),
underscoring that explicit digital requirements in online postings are
overwhelmingly non-trivial.
:::

:::: {.section-card}
#### Country-specific digital skills and knowledge

To identify digital skills and knowledge that are particularly
characteristic of individual countries, we apply the log-likelihood
ratio (`G²`) test following <a class="paper-link" href="https://aclanthology.org/J93-1003.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Dunning (1993)<span class="source-link-ext">↗</span><span class="paper-tooltip" role="tooltip">Dunning, T. (1993). <em>Accurate Methods for the Statistics of Surprise and Coincidence.</em> <em>Computational Linguistics</em>, 19(1), 61&#8211;74.</span></a>, a method widely used in
corpus-based analysis to compare term distributions across text
collections. Here, the same logic is applied to extracted ESCO concepts:
the test assesses whether specific digital concepts (skills and
knowledge) occur in a given country’s job postings in a systematically
different way than would be expected based on their distribution in the
other analysed countries.

The digital concepts are defined using the ESCO digital skills
collection, which identifies 1,284 ESCO concepts associated with
digital competencies. Higher `G²` values indicate concepts that are
more distinctive for the selected country relative to the rest of the
sample. Click on a concept name to reveal its full ESCO definition.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

digitalKeyness = FileAttachment("digital_keyness.json").json()

{
  const data = await digitalKeyness;
  const eaCountries = [
    "Japan","South Korea","Singapore","Taiwan",
    "Malaysia","Thailand","Indonesia","Vietnam","Philippines",
  ].filter(c => data.countries[c]);
  const bmCountries = ["India","Mexico","Poland"].filter(c => data.countries[c]);
  const countryOrder = [...eaCountries, ...bmCountries];

  let selectedCountry = countryOrder[0];
  let showAll = false;
  const INITIAL = 20;

  const wrap = d3.create("div")
    .style("font-family","system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif")
    .style("font-size","13px");

  const controls = wrap.append("div").attr("class", "compare-controls");
  const control = controls.append("div").attr("class", "compare-control");
  control.append("label").text("Select country");
  const buttonGroup = control.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-wrap");

  const eaGroup2 = buttonGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group");
  eaGroup2.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-label").text("East Asia");
  const eaPills2 = eaGroup2.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-pills");

  const bmGroup2 = buttonGroup.append("div").attr("class", "country-group");
  bmGroup2.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-label").text("Benchmark");
  const bmPills2 = bmGroup2.append("div").attr("class", "country-group-pills");

  wrap.append("div").attr("class","compare-note")
    .text("Ranked by G² log-likelihood ratio. Click a concept name to see its ESCO definition.");

  const meta = wrap.append("div").attr("class","keyness-meta");
  const tableHost = wrap.append("div");

  function render() {
    const countryMeta = data.countries[selectedCountry];
    const totalMentions = (countryMeta.digital_skill_mentions || 0)
      + (countryMeta.digital_knowledge_mentions || 0);
    meta.text(`${selectedCountry}: ${d3.format(",")(totalMentions)} digital concept mentions across ${d3.format(",")(countryMeta.offers)} postings.`);

    tableHost.html("");
    const allRows = data.merged[selectedCountry] || [];
    const visibleRows = showAll ? allRows : allRows.slice(0, INITIAL);

    const tbl = tableHost.append("table")
      .style("border-collapse","collapse").style("width","100%");

    const hdr = tbl.append("thead").append("tr")
      .style("border-bottom","2px solid #e2e8f0");
    [["#","30px","left"],["Concept","","left"],["Code","80px","left"],
     ["Mentions","70px","right"],["Per 1k","60px","right"],
     ["Rest","60px","right"],["G²","70px","right"]].forEach(([label,w,align]) => {
      const th = hdr.append("th").style("padding","6px 6px")
        .style("font-size","11px").style("color","#64748b")
        .style("text-align",align).text(label);
      if (w) th.style("width",w);
    });

    const tbody = tbl.append("tbody");
    visibleRows.forEach((row, i) => {
      const bg = i % 2 === 0 ? "#f8fafc" : "#fff";
      const tr = tbody.append("tr").style("background",bg);

      tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 6px").style("font-size","11px")
        .style("color","#94a3b8").style("text-align","left")
        .style("font-variant-numeric","tabular-nums").text(i + 1);

      const conceptTd = tr.append("td").style("padding","4px 6px");
      const labelDiv = conceptTd.append("div")
```
